Al-Ameri's father Sheikh Musallam bin Salem bin Ham Al Ameri and his grandfather, Sheikh Salem bin Ham, played formative roles in the early years of the UAE. Al-Ameri traveled to the United States to obtain a master's degree from the Grand Canyon University, and in 2008 received a doctorate degree from the University of Kent.

In 2005, he was appointed Vice Chairman of Bin Ham Group, a national economic group. In 2011, he was elected to be a member of the Federal National Council. In 2012, he was chosen as one of the founding members of the Association, 'We are all UAE'. He is the Deputy Secretary-General of the Intergovernmental Institution for the use of Micro-algae Spirulina against Malnutrition (IIMSAM), Deputy Chairman of the Bin Ham Group and Member of Al Ain Municipal Council. Al-Ameri joined The Giving Pledge, committing to donate at least half his wealth to charity alongside 215 other signatories from 24 countries.
achievements: Joined The Giving Pledge to donate half his wealth to charity
Elected to UAE Federal National Council in 2011
Published three books including 'United Arab Emirates - Wise Governance'
Received Mohammad Bin Rashid Medal for Scientific Excellence 2008 and 2011
Selected as best Arab cultural figure for 2019

figures.name: Musallam bin Salem bin Ham Al Ameri
figures.title: Sheikh
figures.roleDescription: Father of Mohammed bin Musallam al-Ameri, played formative role in early UAE
figures.significance: Played a formative role in the early years of the UAE as part of the Al-Ameri family's involvement in the nation's development.
figures.biography: Sheikh Musallam bin Salem bin Ham Al Ameri was the father of Mohammed bin Musallam bin Ham al-Ameri and played a formative role in the early years of the UAE. Along with his father Sheikh Salem bin Ham, he was part of the Al-Ameri family's significant involvement in the founding and development of the United Arab Emirates. The family has been noted for its interest in public service and contribution to the early establishment of the UAE as a nation.

figures.title: Sheikh
figures.roleDescription: Grandfather of Mohammed bin Musallam al-Ameri, played formative role in early UAE
figures.significance: Patriarch of the Al-Ameri family who played a formative role in the early years of the UAE.
figures.biography: Sheikh Salem bin Ham Al-Ameri was the grandfather of Mohammed bin Musallam bin Ham al-Ameri and a patriarch of the Al-Ameri family. He played a formative role in the early years of the United Arab Emirates alongside his son Sheikh Musallam bin Salem bin Ham Al Ameri. The Al-Ameri family has been recognized for its significant contributions to the founding and development of the UAE, with Salem bin Ham being part of the foundational generation that helped establish the nation.

figures.name: Abdulla Al Dowsari
figures.title: Security Official
figures.roleDescription: Government security official involved in alleged torture activities
figures.significance: Named as one of the individuals alleged to have committed or overseen torture during Bahrain's repressive period.
figures.biography:
Abdulla Al Dowsari was identified as one of several individuals alleged to have committed or overseen torture during Bahrain's period of systematic human rights violations. He served in a security capacity during the era when the State Security Act of 1974 was in force, a time when torture was considered routine practice in Bahrain between 1975 and 1999.

Al Dowsari's name appears alongside other security officials including Ian Henderson, Adel Flaifel, Khalid Al Wazzan, Sheikh Abdulaziz Ateyatallah Al-Khalifa, and Alistair Bain McNutt who were all alleged to have been involved in torture activities. Despite the well-documented practice of torture during this period and the specific allegations against these individuals, no alleged perpetrator has been tried for torture or ill treatment.

The period during which Al Dowsari operated was marked by particularly severe human rights violations, especially during the 1990s Uprising between 1994 and 1997. The systematic nature of torture during this time included methods such as severe beatings, suspension in contorted positions, sleep deprivation, and various forms of psychological abuse. The blanket amnesty provided by Decree 56 of 2002 effectively protected Al Dowsari and other security officials from prosecution for human rights violations committed prior to 2001.

figures.name: Suliman S. Olayan
figures.title: Founder of The Olayan Group
figures.roleDescription: Entrepreneur and founder of multinational business empire
figures.significance: Founded The Olayan Group in 1947, which became a major multinational enterprise with operations worth over $10 billion.
figures.biography:
Sheikh Sulaiman Al-Olayan was a Saudi entrepreneur who founded The Olayan Group in 1947, establishing what would become one of the region's most successful business empires. The group grew into a private multinational enterprise engaged in distribution, manufacturing, services and investments, with operations spanning the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia and the Middle East. Under his leadership, the company developed partnerships with leading multinationals and became one of the largest investors in Saudi and regional stock markets.

Olayan's business acumen and vision transformed the company from its modest beginnings into a diversified conglomerate. By the time of his death, the private family fortune was estimated to top $10 billion. His legacy continued through his children, particularly his daughter Lubna, who became CEO of Olayan Financing Company. The Olayan Group's influence extended beyond business, with significant investments in education, infrastructure, and various sectors across the Middle East.
achievements: Founded The Olayan Group in 1947
Built multinational enterprise worth over $10 billion
Established partnerships with leading international companies
Created one of the largest investment portfolios in Saudi Arabia and the region

figures.name: Lubna Suliman al-Olayan
figures.title: Former CEO of Olayan Financing Company
figures.roleDescription: Prominent Saudi businesswoman and banking executive
figures.significance: First Saudi woman to head a bank and former CEO of Olayan Financing Company, one of the most powerful women in global business.
figures.biography:
Lubna Suliman al-Olayan was born on August 4, 1955, in Khobar, in Saudi Arabia's Eastern Province, as the youngest child of Sheikh Sulaiman Al-Olayan, founder of The Olayan Group. She obtained a Bachelor of Science in Agriculture from Cornell University and a Master's degree in Business Administration from Indiana University. After working at Morgan Guaranty Trust Bank in New York City from 1979 to 1981, she returned to Saudi Arabia to join the family business.

She served as CEO of Olayan Financing Company (OFC) until May 2019, overseeing the holding entity for The Olayan Group's operations in Saudi Arabia and the Middle East. Under her leadership, OFC operated or participated in more than 40 companies and became one of the largest investors in Saudi and regional stock markets. In June 2019, she made history by becoming the first Saudi woman to head a bank when she was named Chairwoman of the Saudi British Bank (SABB), a position she has held through multiple re-elections.

Beyond her corporate role, Lubna has served on numerous prestigious boards including WPP, Citigroup's International Advisory Board, and Rolls-Royce. She has been a trustee of Cornell University, a board member of INSEAD, and serves on the Board of Trustees of the World Economic Forum. Forbes has consistently ranked her among the world's most powerful women, listing her as the 86th most powerful woman globally as of 2014.
achievements: First Saudi woman to head a bank (SABB)
Former CEO of Olayan Financing Company
Served on boards of major international companies including WPP, Citigroup, and Rolls-Royce
Ranked among Forbes' most powerful women globally
Cornell Entrepreneur of the Year (2010)
Member of MIT Corporation and various prestigious international boards

figures.name: Khaled Olayan
figures.title: Board Member of The Olayan Group
figures.roleDescription: Family member and board member of The Olayan Group
figures.significance: Serves on the board of The Olayan Group alongside his sisters, continuing the family's business legacy.
figures.biography:
Khaled Olayan is the son of Sheikh Sulaiman Al-Olayan, founder of The Olayan Group, and brother to Lubna Olayan. He serves on the board of The Olayan Group along with his sisters Lubna, Hayat, and Hutham, representing the second generation of family leadership in the multinational business empire.

As a board member, Khaled plays a role in overseeing the group's diverse operations across distribution, manufacturing, services, and investments. The Olayan family's collective involvement in the business has helped maintain the company's position as one of the region's most successful private enterprises, with the family fortune estimated to exceed $10 billion.

As a board member, Hayat contributes to the strategic oversight of the group's extensive operations across various sectors including distribution, manufacturing, services, and investments. figures.name: Ghazi Abdul Rahman Al Gosaibi
figures.title: Minister, Diplomat, Poet and Novelist
figures.roleDescription: Saudi politician, diplomat, technocrat, poet, and novelist
figures.significance: Considered among Saudi Arabia's topmost technocrats and called the 'Godfather of Renovation', he served in multiple ministerial positions and was a prominent literary figure.
figures.biography:
Ghazi Abdul Rahman Al Gosaibi was born on 3 March 1940 to one of the richest families of the Kingdom in Hofuf, Al Ahsa province. The Al Gosaibi family was of Najdi origin and among the oldest and richest trading families of Saudi Arabia and Bahrain. His mother was from the Kateb family of Mecca and died when he was nine months old, after which he was raised by his grandmother. He received his primary and secondary education in Bahrain, graduated with a law degree from the University of Cairo in 1961, earned a degree in international relations from the University of Southern California in 1964, and completed his PhD in law at University College London in 1970.

Al Gosaibi began his career as a lecturer at King Saud University in 1965 and was appointed minister of industry and electricity by King Khalid in October 1975, serving until 1983. During this period, he proposed the creation of SABIC (Saudi Basic Industries Corporation) in 1976 and served as its chairman. He also served as minister of health (1983-1984), ambassador to Bahrain (1984-1992), ambassador to the United Kingdom and Ireland (1992-2002), minister of water and electricity (2002-2004), and minister of labor (2004-2010). He was dismissed twice from positions due to his outspoken criticism of corruption and controversial poetry. Al Gosaibi was also a prolific writer, publishing nearly 40 books including novels, poetry collections, and non-fiction works. He died of colon cancer on 15 August 2010 at age 70.
achievements: Founded and chaired Saudi Basic Industries Corporation (SABIC)
Served as Minister of Industry and Electricity, Health, Water and Electricity, and Labor
Served as Ambassador to Bahrain and the United Kingdom
Published nearly 40 books including novels and poetry collections
Received King Abdul Aziz Medal in 1984
Promoted Saudization policies as Minister of Labor

figures.name: Hamad Ahmad Al Gosaibi
figures.title: Business Founder
figures.roleDescription: Founder of Ahmad Hamad Al Gosaibi & Brothers
figures.significance: Founded the Al Gosaibi business empire in the late 1940s, establishing one of the most respected business houses in the Middle East.
figures.biography:
Hamad Ahmad Al Gosaibi founded the family business empire in the late 1940s, originally as a trading company offering money exchange services in the growing coastal town of Al Khobar. His three sons, Ahmad, Abdulaziz and Sulaiman, came together to establish 'Ahmad Hamad Algosaibi and Bros.' (AHAB), expanding the operations significantly beyond the original trading business.

Under the leadership established by Hamad Ahmad Al Gosaibi, the company expanded to include some of the world's most lucrative Pepsi Cola bottling franchises, oilfield services for oil giant Aramco, real estate, hospitality, food & beverages, and investments in banking, insurance, shipping, trading, finance, and manufacturing. The business was reorganized into a holding company and became among the most respected business houses in the Middle East.
achievements: Founded Ahmad Hamad Al Gosaibi & Brothers in the late 1940s
Established original trading and money exchange operations in Al Khobar
Created the foundation for one of the Middle East's most respected business houses

figures.name: Ahmad Hamad Al Gosaibi
figures.title: Business Leader
figures.roleDescription: Co-founder and leading partner of Ahmad Hamad Al Gosaibi & Brothers
figures.significance: One of the three founding brothers who built Ahmad Hamad Al Gosaibi & Brothers into a major Middle Eastern business conglomerate.
figures.biography:
Ahmad Hamad Al Gosaibi was one of the three sons of Hamad Ahmad Al Gosaibi who came together to establish 'Ahmad Hamad Algosaibi and Bros.' (AHAB). Along with his brothers Abdulaziz and Sulaiman, he expanded the company's operations significantly from the original trading business to include diverse sectors. The company grew to encompass Pepsi Cola bottling franchises, oilfield services for Saudi Aramco, real estate, hospitality, food & beverages, and investments in banking, insurance, shipping, trading, finance, and manufacturing.

Ahmad died in 1991, and his equity in the general partnership was transferred to his sons Yousef Ahmad Al Gosaibi, Abdulmohsin Ahmad Al Gosaibi, Khalid Ahmad Al Gosaibi, and three of his daughters. Each of the three founding brothers owned one third of AHAB, making the Al Gosaibi Group a significant family-owned business empire in the Middle East.
achievements: Co-founded and expanded Ahmad Hamad Al Gosaibi & Brothers
Developed major Pepsi Cola bottling franchises
Established oilfield services operations for Saudi Aramco
Built diverse business portfolio across multiple sectors

figures.name: Abdulaziz Hamad Al Gosaibi
figures.title: Business Leader
figures.roleDescription: Co-founder and leading partner of Ahmad Hamad Al Gosaibi & Brothers
figures.significance: One of the three founding brothers who built Ahmad Hamad Al Gosaibi & Brothers into a major Middle Eastern business conglomerate.
figures.biography:
Abdulaziz Hamad Al Gosaibi was one of the three sons of Hamad Ahmad Al Gosaibi who came together to establish 'Ahmad Hamad Algosaibi and Bros.' (AHAB). Along with his brothers Ahmad and Sulaiman, he played a key role in expanding the company's operations from the original trading business established by their father in the late 1940s. The company grew to include diverse sectors such as Pepsi Cola bottling franchises, oilfield services for Saudi Aramco, real estate, hospitality, food & beverages, and investments in banking, insurance, shipping, trading, finance, and manufacturing.

Abdulaziz died in 2003, and his equity in the general partnership was transferred to his wife, son Saud, and his six daughters. The three founding brothers each owned one third of AHAB, establishing the Al Gosaibi Group as one of the most respected business houses in the Middle East with diverse corporate partnerships and manufacturing operations throughout the region.
achievements: Co-founded and expanded Ahmad Hamad Al Gosaibi & Brothers
Developed business operations across multiple sectors
Established corporate partnerships with international companies
Built manufacturing operations throughout the Middle East

figures.name: Sulaiman Hamad Al Gosaibi
figures.title: Business Leader
figures.roleDescription: Co-founder and leading partner of Ahmad Hamad Al Gosaibi & Brothers
figures.significance: The youngest of the three founding brothers who built Ahmad Hamad Al Gosaibi & Brothers into a major Middle Eastern business conglomerate.
figures.biography:
Sulaiman Hamad Al Gosaibi was the youngest of the three sons of Hamad Ahmad Al Gosaibi who came together to establish 'Ahmad Hamad Algosaibi and Bros.' (AHAB). Along with his brothers Ahmad and Abdulaziz, he helped expand the company's operations significantly from the original trading business to encompass a wide range of sectors including Pepsi Cola bottling franchises, oilfield services for Saudi Aramco, real estate, hospitality, food & beverages, and investments in banking, insurance, shipping, trading, finance, and manufacturing.

Sulaiman died in February 2009, and his equity in the general partnership was transferred to his son Dawood Sulaiman Algosaibi and his two daughters. As one of the three founding brothers who each owned one third of AHAB, Sulaiman played a crucial role in building the Al Gosaibi Group into among the most respected business houses in the Middle East, with corporate partnerships including companies like Jotun and Crown Holdings in manufacturing operations throughout the region.
achievements: Co-founded Ahmad Hamad Al Gosaibi & Brothers as youngest founding brother
Developed extensive business operations across multiple sectors
Established corporate partnerships with international companies
Built one of the Middle East's most respected business conglomerates

figures.name: Ibrahim al-Muhaidib
figures.title: Former President of Al-Nassr FC
figures.roleDescription: Former president of Al-Nassr Football Club who resigned in 2024
figures.significance: Former president of Al-Nassr FC who resigned due to lack of control over the club during the Cristiano Ronaldo era.
figures.biography:
Ibrahim al-Muhaidib served as president of Al-Nassr Football Club during a critical period in the club's history. His tenure coincided with the arrival of global superstar Cristiano Ronaldo in December 2022, which brought unprecedented international attention to the Saudi club.

Despite the club's high-profile signings and significant investment, Al-Muhaidib's presidency faced challenges during the 2023-24 season. The club finished second in the league with 82 points (the highest in club history) and Ronaldo won the golden boot, but they failed to win any domestic titles and were knocked out of the AFC Champions League in the quarter-finals. Fan frustration over these failures led to pressure on the club's leadership.

In 2024, al-Muhaidib resigned from his position as president, citing insufficient control over the club's operations. His resignation came at the start of the 2024-25 season amid continued fan anger over the club's inability to convert their significant investments into trophy success.

figures.name: Ibrahim Alfardan
figures.title: Founder of Alfardan Group
figures.roleDescription: Business patriarch and founder of the Alfardan Group
figures.significance: Founded the Alfardan Group in 1954, establishing one of Qatar's prominent business conglomerates.
figures.biography:
Ibrahim Alfardan was the grandfather of composer Dana Al Fardan and the founding patriarch of the Al Fardan business dynasty. He established the Alfardan Group in 1954, which would become a major business conglomerate in Qatar. The company started as a trading business and expanded over the decades into various sectors including automotive, jewelry, real estate, and other commercial ventures.

As the founder and patriarch, Ibrahim Alfardan laid the foundation for what would become one of Qatar's most successful family businesses. His entrepreneurial vision in the 1950s, during Qatar's early development phase, positioned the family as prominent merchants and business leaders in the region. The Alfardan Group's success under his leadership established the family's reputation in Qatar's business community and provided the foundation for subsequent generations to build upon.
achievements: Founded the Alfardan Group in 1954
Established family business dynasty in Qatar

figures.name: Ali Al Fardan
figures.title: Business Leader
figures.roleDescription: Second generation leader of the Alfardan Group and father of Dana Al Fardan
figures.significance: Continued the family business legacy as part of the second generation of the Alfardan Group leadership.
figures.biography:
Ali Al Fardan is the son of Ibrahim Alfardan, the founder of the Alfardan Group, and represents the second generation of the family business dynasty. He is married to Shafiqa Habib and is the father of six daughters, including renowned composer Dana Al Fardan. As part of the family business leadership, he has been involved in continuing the operations and expansion of the Alfardan Group that his father established in 1954.

Ali Al Fardan has maintained the family's prominence in Qatar's business community, overseeing the family enterprise during its continued growth and diversification. His role in the family business has provided the foundation for his children, particularly Dana, to pursue their own careers while maintaining connections to the family's commercial interests. The family business success under his involvement has allowed the next generation to explore various fields including the arts and music.
achievements: Continued family business legacy
Father of prominent composer Dana Al Fardan

figures.name: Dana Al Fardan
figures.title: Composer and Cultural Ambassador
figures.roleDescription: First female Qatari contemporary composer and cultural ambassador
figures.significance: First female Qatari contemporary composer and the first Qatari woman to sing in English, serving as cultural ambassador for the Qatar Philharmonic Orchestra.
figures.biography:
Dana Al Fardan was born on July 29, 1985, in Doha, Qatar, as the daughter of Ali Al Fardan and Shafiqa Habib, and granddaughter of Ibrahim Alfardan, founder of the Alfardan Group. She is the eldest of six sisters and initially pursued international relations at the American University of Sharjah, followed by gemology studies in London, before working in the family business. However, in 2012, during her third trimester of pregnancy, she decided to pursue her true calling in music and recorded her first album in London.

Her debut album 'Paint' was released in 2013, followed by the orchestral album 'Sandstorm' in 2017. She achieved international recognition with her West End musical 'Broken Wings' in 2018, adapting Khalil Gibran's novel, and later created the musical 'Rumi' in 2021. Al Fardan has broken multiple barriers as the first female Qatari contemporary composer and the first Qatari woman to sing in English, while serving as cultural ambassador for the Qatar Philharmonic Orchestra. Her work blends classical and contemporary music with Arabic influences, and she has been recognized for humanitarian work in Gaza and won 'Woman of the Year' at the Grazia Style Awards 2017.
achievements: First female Qatari contemporary composer
First Qatari woman to sing in English
Cultural ambassador for Qatar Philharmonic Orchestra
Debut album 'Paint' (2013)
Orchestral album 'Sandstorm' (2017)
West End musical 'Broken Wings' (2018)

figures.name: Ahmed Al Fardan
figures.title: Rally Driver
figures.roleDescription: Bahraini rally driver and motorsport manager
figures.significance: Established Bahrain's presence in regional motorsport and managed his son's international racing career.
figures.biography:
Ahmed Al Fardan is a Bahraini former rally driver who competed in national and regional rallying championships across the Middle East. He was a regular participant in the Bahrain National Rally Championship and events organized under the Bahrain Motor Federation (BMF). His active racing years coincided with the growth of international motorsport across the Middle East, where he participated in regional events such as the Bahrain International Rally and other rounds of the FIA Middle East Rally Championship.

Following his retirement from active competition, Al Fardan transitioned into a managerial and advisory role, becoming instrumental in managing the career of his son, Hamad Al Fardan, guiding him through various international open-wheel categories including Formula BMW Asia, British Formula 3, and the GP2 Asia Series. He was also known for his involvement with Gulf Finance House (GFH) racing initiatives, which sponsored Bahraini drivers on the international stage to bridge the gap between regional racing and global platforms like Formula 1.
achievements: Competed in Bahrain National Rally Championship
Participated in FIA Middle East Rally Championship
Managed son Hamad's international racing career
Involved with Gulf Finance House racing initiatives

figures.name: Hamad Al Fardan
figures.title: Racing Driver
figures.roleDescription: First Bahraini driver to compete at GP2 Series level
figures.significance: First Bahraini racing driver to compete at GP2 Series level, pioneering Bahraini participation in international motorsport.
figures.biography:
Hamad Al Fardan was born on June 29, 1987, in Manama, Bahrain, as the son of rally driver Ahmed Al Fardan. He began his formula racing career in 2004 with the Formula BMW Asia series, where he achieved significant success. In 2005, he completed a full season with the Malaysian team Meritus and finished third overall, also competing in the Formula BMW World Final. His career progressed through various international series including Formula Renault, Toyota Racing Series in New Zealand, and British Formula 3.

Al Fardan made history in 2008 when he signed with iSport International to compete in the GP2 Asia Series, becoming the first Bahraini and Arabian driver to race at this level. His participation was backed by Gulf Finance House, which led to the team being renamed GFH Team iSport for the duration of the championship. Throughout his career, he competed across multiple continents, from Asia to Europe to New Zealand, winning races in Formula BMW Asia, Formula Asia V6 Renault, and the New Zealand Grand Prix. His achievements helped establish Bahrain's presence in international motorsport before the country hosted its first Formula 1 Grand Prix in 2004.
achievements: First Bahraini to compete in GP2 Series
Third place in Formula BMW Asia (2005)
Won New Zealand Grand Prix (2006)
Formula Asia V6 Renault champion (2009)
Multiple race winner across various series

figures.name: Yusuf bin Ahmed Kanoo
figures.title: Founder
figures.roleDescription: Merchant and trader who founded the Kanoo Group trading empire
figures.significance: Founded the Yusuf Bin Ahmed Kanoo Group in 1890, which became one of the largest independent trading companies in the Gulf region.
figures.biography:
Yusuf bin Ahmed Kanoo was born in Bahrain in 1868, growing up in Manama. From an early age, he worked in his father Ahmed bin Mohamed Kanoo's small foodstuffs trading company. Taking over the business from his father in 1890 at age 22, he chartered boats to import commodities directly from Kerala and Mombasa, expanding links to the Indian coast and East African ports. For the rest of his life, Kanoo made annual trading voyages to India for four to six months.

Recognizing the importance of British connections, Kanoo took an unpaid post in 1898 with the first British Resident Agent in Bahrain to learn English and familiarize himself with British operations. By 1900, he acted as interpreter for British officials with the Ruler of Bahrain. He built a significant regional shipping and trading business, securing agencies for Arab Steamers Ltd in 1911 and APOC in 1913. He introduced numerous innovations to Bahrain including the country's first bank vault in 1913, the first petrol pump in 1929, and various banking services.

Despite receiving British honors including the Kaiser-i-Hind Medal (1912), MBE (1919), and CIE (1923), his relationship with British authorities was often strained. The collapse of the pearling industry by 1930 forced him into debt, requiring loans from the Eastern Bank. Though he repaid most debts by his death on December 21, 1945, he left his two adopted sons Jassim and Ali only 10,000 Rupees but also a collection of lucrative businesses and agencies that would evolve into a major conglomerate.
achievements: Founded the Yusuf Bin Ahmed Kanoo Group in 1890
Established Bahrain's first bank vault in 1913
Set up Bahrain's first petrol station in 1929
Awarded Kaiser-i-Hind Medal (1912), MBE (1919), and CIE (1923) by the British
Built extensive shipping and trading agency network across the Gulf

figures.name: Mishal Hamed Kanoo
figures.title: Chairman
figures.roleDescription: Chairman of The Kanoo Group and prominent UAE businessman
figures.significance: Current chairman of The Kanoo Group following Yusuf Ahmed Kanoo's death in 2014, leading the conglomerate's operations across diverse sectors.
figures.biography:
Mishal Hamed Kanoo was born on February 25, 1969, in Dubai and educated through high school locally before continuing college studies in the United States. He earned a double major in Comparative Theology and Philosophy and Economics as his first degree, later obtaining his MBA in Finance from the University of St. Thomas in Houston. After leaving the business temporarily, he earned a second MBA at the American University of Sharjah, where he subsequently became a visiting lecturer on Family Business.

Kanoo worked at Arthur Andersen in Dubai as an auditor before returning to The Kanoo Group as Deputy Chairman in 1997, serving under his uncle Mubarak Kanoo who was then chairman of the holding company. In 2015, he assumed the Chairmanship of The Kanoo Group following the passing of Yusuf Ahmed Kanoo in 2014. Under his leadership, the company operates across diverse sectors including shipping, travel, machinery, logistics, property, energy, chemicals, training, retail, and various joint ventures.

Beyond the family business, Kanoo holds chairman or director positions in multiple companies including Dubai Express LLC/Freightworks, Johnson Arabia LLC, Wolffkran Arabia LLC, and Gulf Capital Pvt. JSC. He is actively involved in Dubai's art scene, co-founding the Meem Gallery in 2007 with Sultan Sooud Al-Qassemi and Charles Pocock. The gallery specializes in modern and contemporary Arab and Iranian art and houses the Noor Library of Middle Eastern Art, one of the largest international resource centers for Middle Eastern arts.
achievements: Assumed chairmanship of The Kanoo Group in 2015
Featured in Top 100 Powerful Arabs 2017, Power 100, and Rich List 2009
Co-founded Meem Gallery in Dubai in 2007
Serves as visiting lecturer at American University of Sharjah
Holds chairman/director positions in over 10 major companies

figures.name: Abdulaziz Jassim Kanoo
figures.title: Vice-Chairman
figures.roleDescription: Vice-Chairman and Deputy CEO of Yusuf Bin Ahmed Kanoo Company
figures.significance: Prominent Bahraini businessman who held key leadership positions across major Gulf companies and was awarded Bahrain's highest civilian honor.
figures.biography:
Abdulaziz Jassim Kanoo was born in 1931 into the famed Kanoo merchant family. He attended primary and secondary school in Somerset, England, before enrolling at the American University of Beirut and earning a business degree in Scotland. Upon returning to the Gulf region, he assumed a leadership role in the family business as Vice-Chairman and Deputy CEO of Yusuf Bin Ahmed Kanoo Company.

Throughout his career, Kanoo held numerous prestigious positions across major Gulf corporations. He served as Chairman of Al Jazeera Tourism Company, Saudi Corporation for Industry and Commerce, Saudi Loading Company, and Novotel Bahrain Al Dana Resort. He also held the position of Vice-Chairman at Investcorp and served on the boards of directors for major companies including United Arab Shipping Company, Saudi Public Transport Company (SAPTCO), and Public Bank Berhad. His involvement extended to public service, serving on the boards of the Saudi Arabian General Investment Authority for the Eastern Province and the Human Rights Commission in Saudi Arabia.

Kanoo married his cousin Sarah Ali Kanoo and had four children: Ali, Saud, Nawf, and Badr. His brother Abdul Latif Jassim Kanoo founded the renowned Beit Al Quran museum. In recognition of his contributions to business and society, King Hamad bin Isa Al Khalifa awarded him the King Hamad Order of the Renaissance in 2007, Bahrain's highest civilian honor. He was also recognized as the first businessman in the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia in 1994. Kanoo died on August 31, 2016, at age 85 at his home in Mahooz, Capital Governorate, and was buried in Manama Cemetery.
achievements: Awarded King Hamad Order of the Renaissance (2007), Bahrain's highest civilian honor
Named first businessman in the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia (1994)
Served as Vice-Chairman of Investcorp
Held chairman positions at multiple major Gulf corporations
Served on boards of major regional companies and government authorities

figures.name: Abdul Latif Jassim Kanoo
figures.title: Founder
figures.roleDescription: Founder of Beit Al Quran museum and collector of Islamic art
figures.significance: Founded the Beit Al Quran in 1990, which became one of the most renowned Islamic museums in the world.
figures.biography:
Abdul Latif Jassim Kanoo was an avid collector of Quranic manuscripts and Islamic art who felt a strong sense of responsibility toward the rare manuscripts he had acquired over the years. As his collection grew, he came to feel that these historic artifacts required proper preservation and public access. This led him to conceive of establishing a first-of-its-kind institution dedicated to the service of the Quran and preservation of historic manuscripts.

Construction of the Beit Al Quran complex began in 1984, and Kanoo officially opened the museum in March 1990. The concept of a comprehensive institution housing Quranic manuscripts and Islamic art was unique in the Persian Gulf region at the time. Kanoo donated his entire personal collection of Quranic manuscripts and Islamic art to form the core of the museum's holdings, which represents various calligraphic traditions from the first Hijri century to the present day.

The establishment was funded completely by public donations, with contributions from people across all walks of life in Bahrain, from heads of state to school children. The complex includes not only the museum but also a mosque, library, auditorium, and madrasa. The Beit Al Quran houses an internationally celebrated collection of historic Quranic manuscripts from across the Islamic world, from China in the East to Spain in the West, making it one of the most important repositories of Islamic manuscripts globally.
achievements: Founded Beit Al Quran museum in 1990
Created one of the world's most renowned Islamic museums
Donated extensive personal collection of Quranic manuscripts and Islamic art
Established unique concept of comprehensive Quranic institution in the Gulf region

figures.name: Ahmed bin Mohamed Kanoo
figures.title: Patriarch
figures.roleDescription: Early patriarch who established the family's trading business
figures.significance: Father of Yusuf bin Ahmed Kanoo who established the original small foodstuffs trading company that would become the Kanoo Group.
figures.biography:
Ahmed bin Mohamed Kanoo was the patriarch who established the foundational trading business that would eventually grow into the Yusuf Bin Ahmed Kanoo Group. He operated a small foodstuffs trading company in Bahrain, where his son Yusuf bin Ahmed Kanoo worked from an early age, learning the fundamentals of trade and commerce.

When Yusuf took over the business from his father in 1890 at the age of 22, Ahmed's small trading operation served as the launching point for what would become one of the largest independent trading companies in the Gulf region. The business foundation that Ahmed established in foodstuffs trading provided the platform for Yusuf's subsequent expansion into shipping, importing commodities from Kerala and Mombasa, and developing extensive trade networks across the Indian coast and East African ports.
achievements: Established the original Kanoo family trading business
Provided foundation for what became the Kanoo Group empire

figures.name: Sarah Ali Kanoo
figures.title: Matriarch
figures.roleDescription: Wife of Abdulaziz Jassim Kanoo and mother of four children
figures.significance: Matriarch of the Kanoo family who married her cousin Abdulaziz Jassim Kanoo and raised four children who continue the family legacy.
figures.biography:
Sarah Ali Kanoo was a member of the extended Kanoo family who married her cousin Abdulaziz Jassim Kanoo, strengthening the family bonds within the merchant dynasty. Together, they raised four children: Ali, Saud, Nawf, and Badr, who represent the next generation of the Kanoo family.

As the wife of one of the most prominent businessmen in the Gulf region and a matriarch of the Kanoo family, Sarah played an important role in maintaining family unity and traditions. She survived her husband Abdulaziz, who died in August 2016, but passed away herself on January 8, 2018. She was buried in Manama Cemetery, joining other members of the Kanoo family who had contributed to Bahrain's commercial and social development over more than a century.

figures.name: Marzouq Ali Mohammed Al-Ghanim
figures.title: Former Speaker of the Kuwaiti National Assembly
figures.roleDescription: Politician and Speaker of Kuwait's National Assembly
figures.significance: Former speaker of the Kuwaiti National Assembly who represented the second district and was elected Speaker in 2013.
figures.biography:
Marzouq Al-Ghanim was born on November 3, 1968, and is part of the Al-Ghanim family, which is historically dominant in Kuwait and Bahrain as part of the Bani Utub federation that originally founded Kuwait. He earned a BSc in mechanical engineering from Seattle University and worked for Boubyan Petrochemical Company before being elected to the National Assembly in 2006. He was elected Speaker in 2013.

Throughout his political career, he has taken various positions on key issues. In December 2006, he voted against a bill that would have seen the government write off $27bn of citizens' private debts. As head of the Youth and Sports Committee in 2008, he agreed to reform the Kuwaiti football program in line with FIFA recommendations. He has also been vocal in international affairs, notably telling the Israeli delegation at an Inter-Parliamentary Union meeting in St. Petersburg in 2017 to leave the hall, stating they represented 'the most dangerous form of terrorism: state terrorism.'

Al-Ghanim has held numerous leadership positions including President of the GCC Parliamentary Union (2018-2023), President of the Arab Inter-Parliamentary Union (2013-2016), and various committee chairmanships in the Kuwaiti National Assembly.
achievements: Speaker of the Kuwaiti National Assembly (2013)
President of the GCC Parliamentary Union (2018-2023)
President of the Arab Inter-Parliamentary Union (2013-2016)
Chairman of various National Assembly committees

figures.name: Ali Mohammed Thunayan Al-Ghanim
figures.title: Chairman of Ali Alghanim & Sons Company
figures.roleDescription: Business leader and former chairman of Kuwait Chamber of Commerce
figures.significance: Chairman of Ali Alghanim & Sons Company, a multi-line conglomerate, and former chairman of the Kuwait Chamber of Commerce and Industry.
figures.biography:
Ali Mohammed Thunayan Al-Ghanim is the father of Marzouq Al-Ghanim and a prominent businessman in Kuwait. He serves as the chairman of Ali Alghanim & Sons Company, a multi-line conglomerate which includes the BMW and Land Rover car dealership in Kuwait. His business empire represents one of the major commercial enterprises in the country.

In addition to his business leadership, Ali Al-Ghanim has played a significant role in Kuwait's commercial sector as the former chairman of the Kuwait Chamber of Commerce and Industry. He is married to Faiza al-Kharafi, who is a former rector of Kuwait University and heir to the M. A. Kharafi & Sons conglomerate, creating a powerful alliance between two of Kuwait's most prominent business families.
achievements: Chairman of Ali Alghanim & Sons Company
Former chairman of Kuwait Chamber of Commerce and Industry

figures.name: Fouad Mohammed Thunayan Alghanim
figures.title: Chairman of Fouad Alghanim & Sons Group of Companies
figures.roleDescription: International business leader and conglomerate chairman
figures.significance: Chairman of Fouad Alghanim & Sons Group of Companies, a multi-line conglomerate with operations across the U.S., Europe, Africa and the Middle East.
figures.biography:
Fouad Mohammed Thunayan Alghanim is the paternal uncle of Marzouq Al-Ghanim and serves as chairman of Fouad Alghanim & Sons Group of Companies. His business empire is a multi-line conglomerate consisting of a number of fully and partly owned subsidiary companies with an international presence spanning the United States, Europe, Africa and the Middle East.

As part of the Al-Ghanim family's extensive business network, Fouad Alghanim has helped establish the family as one of the most influential merchant families in Kuwait and the broader Gulf region. His international business operations represent the global expansion of the Al-Ghanim commercial interests beyond their traditional base in Kuwait.
achievements: Chairman of Fouad Alghanim & Sons Group of Companies
Built international conglomerate with operations across multiple continents

figures.name: Abdullatif Muhammad Thunayyan Al-Ghanim
figures.title: First Chairman of the Kuwait Constituent Assembly
figures.roleDescription: Founding political figure and constitutional architect
figures.significance: Served as the first Chairman of the Kuwait Constituent Assembly and led the drafting of Kuwait's Constitution.
figures.biography:
Abdullatif Muhammad Thunayyan Al-Ghanim was born in 1912 in the Qibla district of Kuwait City to a wealthy merchant family. He attended Al-Mubarakiya School and then Al-Ahmadiya School before traveling to India with his father in 1922, where his father ran a business in Karachi and Al-Ghanim learned English. His work in the family business led to extensive travel between Kuwait, India, and Iraq, giving him exposure to various cultural and political influences.

Al-Ghanim's political career began early when he was elected to the first Legislative Council following the June 1938 Kuwaiti general election, and again to the second Legislative Council in 1939. In 1952, he won election to the Municipal Council of Kuwait and became director of his municipality. His most significant contribution came in 1962 when he was elected to the Constituent National Assembly and was unanimously named chairman. As one of five members of the Constitutional Drafting Committee, he chaired the committee that created Kuwait's current Constitution.

Following his constitutional work, Al-Ghanim was nominated by Emir Abdullah Al-Salim Al-Sabah to serve as Minister of Public Health in January 1963, and later as Minister of Public Works in January 1964. He died on March 14, 1988, and was remembered as 'one of Kuwait's most loyal men, who contributed to laying the foundations of the country's political history.'
achievements: First Chairman of the Kuwait Constituent Assembly (1962-1963)
Led drafting of Kuwait's Constitution
Minister of Public Health (1963)
Minister of Public Works (1964)

figures.name: Hassan Galadari
figures.title: Merchant and Family Patriarch
figures.roleDescription: Founding patriarch who established the family business tradition
figures.significance: Founded the Galadari merchant dynasty in 1879, establishing the business tradition that would make the family one of the UAE's most prominent commercial houses.
figures.biography:
Hassan Galadari was a businessman who established the Galadari family's commercial legacy in 1879. As the grandfather of Abdul Latif Galadari, he founded what would become a multi-generational business empire that stretched across the Gulf region. His entrepreneurial foundation laid the groundwork for his descendants to build one of the UAE's most successful business conglomerates.

The business tradition that Hassan Galadari established would later be developed by his son Ibrahim Galadari and grandsons into the modern Galadari Brothers Group, which became a major force in Dubai's economic development during the oil boom of the 1970s.
achievements: Established the Galadari business tradition in 1879

figures.name: Ibrahim Galadari
figures.title: Merchant and Father of the Galadari Brothers
figures.roleDescription: Second-generation patriarch who continued the family business
figures.significance: Father of the three Galadari brothers who founded the modern Galadari Group conglomerate and played a key role in Dubai's economic development.
figures.biography:
Ibrahim Galadari was the son of merchant Hassan Galadari and father to the three brothers who would establish the Galadari Brothers Group. Also known as Ebrahim Galadari, he continued the family's merchant tradition established by his father in the late 19th century. Ibrahim raised his sons in Iran, where Abdul Latif Galadari was born in 1939.

Ibrahim's role was crucial in maintaining the family's commercial legacy between the founding generation of Hassan Galadari and the expansion generation of his three sons: Abdul Rahim, Abdul Latif, and Abdul Wahab. His sons would go on to establish one of the UAE's most successful business conglomerates in the early 1960s.

figures.name: Abdul Rahim E. Galadari
figures.title: Co-founder of Galadari Brothers Group
figures.roleDescription: Elder brother and co-founder of the Galadari business empire
figures.significance: Co-founded the Galadari Brothers Group in the early 1960s and helped build one of the UAE's largest business conglomerates.
figures.biography:
Abdul Rahim E. Galadari was the elder brother of Abdul Latif Galadari and co-founder of the Galadari Brothers Group, established in the early 1960s. Along with his younger brothers Abdul Latif and Abdul Wahab, he built what would become one of the UAE's most successful business conglomerates. The brothers were involved in the gold business, which made them multimillionaires by the 1970s.

Abdul Rahim and his brothers were well-positioned to take advantage of Dubai's 1970s oil boom, establishing major enterprises including the Intercontinental Hotel, Dubai Bank, a Mazda vehicle dealership, the Khaleej Times newspaper, and numerous construction, engineering, and trading companies. His son Mohammed Abdul Rahim Galadari attended Abdul Latif's funeral in 2002, representing the continuing family legacy.
achievements: Co-founded Galadari Brothers Group in early 1960s
Built multimillion-dollar gold business by 1970s
Established major hotels, banks, and media companies

figures.name: Abdul Latif Galadari
figures.title: Co-founder of Galadari Brothers Group
figures.roleDescription: Youngest brother and key figure in building the Galadari business empire
figures.significance: Co-founded the Galadari Brothers Group and established GICC, becoming the world's largest Baskin-Robbins franchisee with nearly 700 stores across the GCC.
figures.biography:
Abdul Latif Galadari was born in 1939 in Iran as the youngest son of Ibrahim Galadari and grandson of merchant Hassan Galadari. He studied in the UAE as a young man before joining his elder brothers Abdul Rahim and Abdul Wahab to found the Galadari Brothers Group in the early 1960s. The brothers initially focused on the gold business, which made them multimillionaires by the 1970s.

Abdul Latif and his brothers capitalized on Dubai's 1970s oil boom by diversifying into multiple sectors. They built the Intercontinental Hotel, opened Dubai Bank, established a Mazda vehicle dealership, started Dubai's first English-language daily newspaper the Khaleej Times, and opened numerous construction, engineering, and trading companies. Abdul Latif's most notable achievement was founding GICC, the flagship company of the Galadari Brothers Group, which became the master franchisee for Baskin-Robbins in the GCC and the world's largest Baskin-Robbins franchisee with close to 700 stores across six countries. He died of a heart attack at his residence in Karachi, Pakistan, on March 9, 2002, at age 62.
achievements: Co-founded Galadari Brothers Group
Established GICC as world's largest Baskin-Robbins franchisee
Founded Dubai's first English-language daily newspaper Khaleej Times
Built Intercontinental Hotel Dubai
Opened Dubai Bank

figures.name: Abdul Wahab E. Galadari
figures.title: Co-founder of Galadari Brothers Group
figures.roleDescription: Third brother and original co-founder who was later bought out
figures.significance: Original co-founder of the Galadari Brothers Group who helped establish the business empire before being bought out by his brothers in 1976.
figures.biography:
Abdul Wahab E. Galadari was one of the three founding brothers of the Galadari Brothers Group, established in the early 1960s alongside his brothers Abdul Rahim and Abdul Latif. The three brothers initially worked together to build their business empire, starting with the gold trade that made them multimillionaires by the 1970s.

In 1976, the brothers underwent a major restructuring when Abdul Wahab was bought out by Abdul Rahim and Abdul Latif, who retained control of the business. This split allowed the remaining two brothers to continue developing what would become one of the UAE's largest conglomerates, while Abdul Wahab pursued separate business interests.
achievements: Co-founded Galadari Brothers Group in early 1960s

figures.name: Mohammed Abdul Rahim Galadari
figures.title: Third Generation Family Member
figures.roleDescription: Son of Abdul Rahim Galadari representing the next generation
figures.significance: Representative of the third generation of the Galadari family, continuing the family's business and social prominence in the UAE.
figures.biography:
Mohammed Abdul Rahim Galadari is the nephew of Abdul Latif Galadari and son of Abdul Rahim Galadari, representing the third generation of the prominent Galadari business family. He attended the funeral of his uncle Abdul Latif Galadari in Dubai in March 2002, demonstrating the continued close family ties and his role as a representative of the younger generation.

As a member of the third generation, Mohammed Abdul Rahim Galadari represents the continuation of the family legacy established by his grandfather Ibrahim and great-grandfather Hassan Galadari in the 19th century.

figures.name: Suhail Galadari
figures.title: Director, Khaleej Times and UAE Cricketer
figures.roleDescription: Son of Abdul Latif Galadari, media executive and former cricketer
figures.significance: Current Director of Khaleej Times newspaper and former UAE cricket team member, representing the third generation of the Galadari family in both business and sports.
figures.biography:
Suhail Galadari is the son of Abdul Latif Galadari and currently serves as Director of the Khaleej Times, the English-language daily newspaper founded by his father and uncles as part of the Galadari Brothers Group empire. He represents the third generation of the family continuing their media interests and business operations.

In addition to his business role, Suhail Galadari has been involved in UAE cricket, being listed among prominent Emirati cricketers. He attended his father's funeral in Dubai in March 2002, and has continued to uphold the family's prominence in both the business and sporting communities of the UAE.
achievements: Director of Khaleej Times newspaper
Former UAE cricket team member

figures.name: Hussain Sajwani
figures.title: CEO and Chairman
figures.roleDescription: Real estate magnate and CEO of DAMAC Properties
figures.significance: Billionaire businessman who built one of the Middle East's largest real estate development companies and acquired iconic fashion brand Roberto Cavalli.
figures.biography:
Hussain Sajwani is a prominent Emirati businessman who serves as CEO of DAMAC Properties, one of the leading luxury real estate developers in the Middle East. He is recognized as the wealthiest Emirati and resides in the exclusive Emirates Hills community in Dubai. Sajwani has been instrumental in developing luxury residential projects throughout the region, including partnerships with high-end brands.

In 2019, Sajwani completed the acquisition of Italian fashion group Roberto Cavalli through his private investment company Vision Investments, saving the struggling luxury brand from financial difficulties. In 2022, his company DAMAC purchased a $120 million plot in Miami on the site of the collapsed Surfside condominium, with plans to launch a real estate division and develop luxury Cavalli-branded residences designed by Zaha Hadid Architects.

Sajwani has been particularly active in capitalizing on global economic shifts, with reports indicating that approximately 15% of his customers constitute Russian nationals, especially following the 2022 Ukraine invasion. His business acumen and strategic investments have made him a key figure in Dubai's transformation into a global luxury destination.
achievements: Built DAMAC Properties into one of the Middle East's largest real estate companies
Acquired Italian luxury fashion brand Roberto Cavalli in 2019
Purchased $120 million Miami development site for luxury Cavalli residences
Recognized as the wealthiest Emirati businessman

figures.name: Amir Mohebi
figures.title: Iranian footballer
figures.roleDescription: Professional football player
figures.significance: Iranian footballer noted in the research as a notable sports figure.
figures.biography: Amir Mohebi is an Iranian footballer born in 1981. He is mentioned in the List of Iranians among notable figures in Iranian sports. As a professional footballer, he represents the contemporary era of Iranian athletics and has contributed to Iran's football scene. His inclusion in the comprehensive list of notable Iranians indicates his recognition within the country's sporting community.

figures.name: Jalil Mohebi
figures.title: Iranian Shia cleric
figures.roleDescription: Religious leader and Islamic scholar
figures.significance: Iranian Shia cleric recognized among the contemporary religious scholars in Iran.
figures.biography: Jalil Mohebi is an Iranian Shia cleric who appears in the comprehensive list of Iranian Shia clergy. As a religious leader, he is part of the clerical establishment in Iran and has contributed to the religious and scholarly community. His inclusion among the notable Iranian Shia clergy indicates his standing within the religious hierarchy and his role in Islamic scholarship and guidance within the Iranian religious system.

figures.name: Abdul Rahman Al-Amoudi
figures.title: Former American Muslim Activist
figures.roleDescription: Founder of American Muslim Council, convicted on terrorism-related charges
figures.significance: Founded the American Muslim Council and was involved in a plot to assassinate Saudi Crown Prince Abdullah.
figures.biography:
Abdul Rahman Al-Amoudi was born in the Province of Eritrea in Ethiopia and moved to the United States in 1979, becoming a U.S. citizen in 1996. Before his arrest, he was one of America's best-known Muslim activists who met with senior Clinton and Bush administration officials and founded the American Muslim Foundation. He played a role in establishing a U.S. Department of Defense Muslim chaplain program and was invited by the Bush White House to speak at a Washington National Cathedral prayer service after 9/11.

In 2003, Al-Amoudi was indicted for illegal dealings with Libya and involvement in a plot financed by Muammar Gaddafi to assassinate Saudi Crown Prince Abdullah. He pleaded guilty in 2004 to three federal criminal charges including violating terrorism-related sanctions and was sentenced to 23 years in prison. He was automatically stripped of his U.S. citizenship following his conviction.
achievements: Founded American Muslim Council
Established U.S. Department of Defense Muslim chaplain program

figures.name: Muhammad Sa'eed Al Amoudi
figures.title: Saudi Arabian Journalist and Literary Critic
figures.roleDescription: Prominent 20th-century Saudi journalist, editor, and member of Consultative Assembly
figures.significance: A pioneering writer in 20th-century Hejaz, known for classical Arabic text editing and literary criticism.
figures.biography:
Muhammad bin Sa'eed Al-Amoudi was born in Mecca in 1905 to a merchant family descended from the Hejazi saint Sa'eed ibn Isa Al-Bakri. After graduating from Al-Falah School, he initially worked in commerce with his father before transitioning to government positions. He headed the editorial board of the General Post and Telegraph Authority and served as editor-in-chief of its magazine until 1971.

From 1951 to 1954, he was a member of the Saudi Consultative Assembly and later served as editor-in-chief of the Muslim World League magazine from 1965 to 1978. He was chosen by the Ministry of Education for various committees and was one of the founding members of the Publishing and Writing Committee. Al-Amoudi was recognized as an inspiring poet and distinguished writer, publishing works on history, poetry, and short stories. He died in Mecca on February 16, 1991, after a long illness at age 86.
achievements: First prize from Al-Hilal magazine for best poem (1933)
Member of Saudi Consultative Assembly (1951-1954)
Published complete works in 3 volumes (2007)

figures.name: Omar Abdulrahman Ahmed Al Raaki Al Amoudi
figures.nameAr: عُمَر عَبْد الرَّحْمٰن أَحْمَد الرَّاقِي الْعَمُودِيّ
figures.title: Former Professional Footballer
figures.roleDescription: Emirati attacking midfielder and winger, formerly of Al Ain and UAE national team
figures.significance: Ranked first among top Asian players in 2012 and considered one of the most promising football talents from the region.
figures.biography:
Omar Abdulrahman was born in Riyadh in 1991 to a working-class family from the Al-Amoudi lineage, descending from 13th century Sufi saint Sa'eed ibn Isa Al-Amoudi. After being spotted by a scout at Al Malaz yard, he joined Al Ain's youth academy in 2008 when his family was granted UAE citizenship. He made his professional debut in 2009 and became a first-team regular despite suffering multiple cruciate ligament injuries.

Throughout his career, Abdulrahman won multiple UAE Pro-League titles with Al Ain and was voted Emirati Player of the Year multiple times. He had a two-week trial with Manchester City in 2012 and later played for Al Hilal, Al Jazira, and Shabab Al-Ahli. Internationally, he represented the UAE in major tournaments including the 2012 Olympics, 2015 AFC Asian Cup, and won the 2013 Arabian Gulf Cup where he was named Player of the Tournament. ESPN FC ranked him first among the top ten Asian players of 2012.
achievements: UAE Pro-League champion multiple times
Arabian Gulf Cup winner and MVP (2013)
AFC Asian Cup third place (2015)
Emirati Player of the Year multiple times
ESPN FC Top Asian Player (2012)

figures.name: Sa'eed ibn Isa Al-Amoudi
figures.title: Sunni Shafi'i Islamic Scholar and Sufi Saint
figures.roleDescription: Prominent Islamic scholar and founder of Tariqah Al-Amoudiyyah
figures.significance: Founded his own Sufi order and exercised significant spiritual influence in Hadhramaut, establishing the religious prominence of the Al-Amoudi family.
figures.biography:
Sheikh Sa'eed ibn Isa al-Amoudi was born in 1203/1204 CE in Qaydun, Wadi Daw'an, Hadhramaut. His father Sheikh Isa ibn Ahmad Al-Amoudi was originally from Medina and served as a Mufti there before migrating to spread Islamic learning. Sa'eed ibn Isa studied under various scholars including learning from the Tariqah of Abu Madyan Shu'ayb Al-Andalusi and was a student of Faqih Al-Muqaddam Sayyid Muhammad ibn Ali Ba'Alawi, founder of the Ba'Alawi Tariqah.

After years of study under different Sufi masters, Sa'eed ibn Isa developed his own method of Tasawwuf known as Tariqah Al-Amoudiyyah, which became one of the twenty-three renowned Sufi ways in Hadhramaut. He was allegedly given custody of the Staff of Moses and Coat of Joseph by his teacher Muhammad ibn Ali Ba'Alawi. Sa'eed ibn Isa died around 1272 CE in Qaydun and was buried next to his mosque. His religious authority was inherited by his descendants, who eventually established political power in the region, founding an independent Al-Amoudi state that lasted from 1531 to 1899 CE.
achievements: Founded Tariqah Al-Amoudiyyah Sufi order
Established Al-Amoudi family's religious prominence in Hadhramaut
Custodian of alleged Staff of Moses and Coat of Joseph

figures.name: Samia al-Amoudi
figures.title: Obstetrician, Gynecologist and Healthcare Activist
figures.roleDescription: Head of Sheikh Mohammed Hussein Al-Amoudi Center of Excellence in breast cancer
figures.significance: First woman from the Gulf Cooperation Council to sit on the Union for International Cancer Control board and pioneering breast cancer awareness advocate.
figures.biography:
Samia al-Amoudi graduated from King Abdulaziz University's medical college in 1981, among the first doctors from that institution. In April 2006, she diagnosed herself with breast cancer and underwent chemotherapy, completing treatment in 2012. She became the first Saudi woman to write publicly about her experience with the disease, publishing a series of articles in Al Madinah newspaper and appearing on daily TV shows to raise awareness.

Since 2012, she has served as head of the Sheikh Mohammed Hussein Al-Amoudi Center of Excellence in breast cancer and scientific chair for breast cancer at King Abdulaziz University. She has been recognized internationally for her advocacy work, receiving the International Women of Courage Award from U.S. Secretary of State Condoleezza Rice in 2007. Arabian Business ranked her among the most powerful Arab women, placing her 5th on their Power 100 list in 2010.
achievements: International Women of Courage Award (2007)
Ranked 5th on Arabian Business Power 100 list (2010)
First Gulf woman on Union for International Cancer Control board
Published 'Breast Cancer, Break the Silence' (2007)

figures.name: Zainal Alireza
figures.title: Founder
figures.roleDescription: Founder of House of Alireza trading enterprise
figures.significance: Founded Saudi Arabia's oldest organized commercial enterprise in 1845.
figures.biography:
Zainal Alireza was the founder of the House of Alireza, established in 1845 and reputed to be Saudi Arabia's oldest organized commercial enterprise. The family arrived in Jeddah in the 1840s, having migrated from Laristan in southern Iran. Zainal started off trading food, textiles, and other merchandise from the Middle East and the Indian subcontinent, and also acted as agents for pilgrims on the hajj for one of the larger British-Indian shipping lines.

The House was an early backer of Abdulaziz al Saud, the first king of Saudi Arabia, establishing the family's long-standing involvement in Saudi Arabian politics since the end of the Ottoman Empire. Zainal's legacy continued through his descendants, who expanded the business into various sectors including real estate, representative agency rights, and joint ventures with construction and engineering companies that contributed to Saudi Arabian infrastructural development.
achievements: Founded House of Alireza in 1845
Established trading networks across Middle East and Indian subcontinent
Early supporter of Abdulaziz al Saud

figures.name: Qasim bin Zainal Alireza
figures.title: Ottoman Parliamentarian
figures.roleDescription: Deputy for Jeddah in Ottoman parliament
figures.significance: First Alireza family member to hold political office, serving as deputy for Jeddah in the Ottoman parliament of 1908.
figures.biography:
Qasim bin Zainal Alireza was the son of the family patriarch Zainal Alireza and represented the family's entry into formal political representation. In 1908, he was elected as the deputy for Jeddah within the Ottoman parliament, marking an important milestone in the family's political involvement during the final years of Ottoman rule in the Hijaz region.

His election to the Ottoman parliament demonstrated the family's growing influence and prominence in Jeddah's commercial and political circles. This political engagement would set a precedent for future generations of the Alireza family, who continued to play significant roles in Saudi Arabian politics and governance after the establishment of the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia.
achievements: Elected deputy for Jeddah in Ottoman parliament of 1908

figures.name: Abdullah Alireza Zainal
figures.title: Kaymakam and Royal Advisor
figures.roleDescription: Governor of Jeddah and senior advisor to King Abdullah
figures.significance: Served as kaymakam (governor) of Jeddah during Hashemite and early al Saud rule, later becoming senior advisor to King Abdullah bin Abdulaziz.
figures.biography:
Abdullah Alireza Zainal, uncle to Qasim bin Zainal Alireza, served as kaymakam (equivalent to governor) of Jeddah during the transitional period from Hashemite to early al Saud rule. This position placed him at the center of the political transformation that saw the establishment of modern Saudi Arabia under King Abdulaziz al Saud.

After his gubernatorial service, Abdullah continued his political career and eventually became a senior advisor to King Abdullah bin Abdulaziz, demonstrating the family's enduring influence in Saudi royal circles. His long career spanning different political eras showcased the family's ability to maintain relevance and trust across changing regimes, contributing to the family's continued prominence in Saudi Arabian politics and business.
achievements: Served as kaymakam (governor) of Jeddah
Senior advisor to King Abdullah bin Abdulaziz

figures.name: Muhammad Alireza
figures.title: Minister of Commerce and Ambassador
figures.roleDescription: Second Minister of Commerce and Saudi ambassador
figures.significance: Served as Saudi Arabia's second Minister of Commerce from 1953-1958 and later as ambassador to Egypt and France.
figures.biography:
Muhammad Alireza, son of Abdullah Alireza Zainal, became one of the most prominent political figures in the Alireza family. He served as Saudi Arabia's second Minister of Commerce from 1953 to 1958, playing a crucial role in developing the kingdom's commercial policies during the early years of the oil boom.

After his ministerial service, Muhammad continued his diplomatic career, serving as Saudi ambassador to Egypt and France. These appointments reflected both his personal capabilities and the royal family's continued trust in the Alireza family. His dual roles in commerce and diplomacy positioned him as a key figure in Saudi Arabia's international economic relations during a critical period of the kingdom's development.
achievements: Second Minister of Commerce of Saudi Arabia (1953-1958)
Saudi ambassador to Egypt
Saudi ambassador to France

figures.name: Muhammad Ali Zainal Alireza
figures.title: Educator and Pearl Merchant
figures.roleDescription: Educational pioneer and international pearl merchant
figures.significance: Established the first schools in Jeddah (1905) and Mecca (1912) for poor children, and became a successful pearl merchant in Paris and Bombay.
figures.biography:
Muhammad Ali Zainal Alireza, another son of Zainal Alireza, was a pioneering figure in education and international commerce. In 1905, he established the first school in Jeddah for poor children, offering free tuition at a time when education was not widely available. He followed this in 1912 by opening another school in Mecca, also providing free education to underprivileged children. As the region was still part of the Ottoman Empire, the language of instruction was Ottoman Turkish.

After establishing these educational institutions, Muhammad Ali expanded his business interests internationally, becoming a successful pearl merchant with operations in Paris and Bombay. He specialized in importing Persian Gulf pearls to both European and Indian markets, demonstrating the family's early international business acumen. Though his schools were eventually closed due to violations of British laws, his educational legacy marked an important early contribution to learning in the Hijaz region.
achievements: Established first school in Jeddah (1905)
Founded school in Mecca (1912)
Successful pearl merchant in Paris and Bombay

figures.name: Abdullah Alireza
figures.title: Founder of Rezayat Group
figures.roleDescription: Founded major Gulf business conglomerate
figures.significance: Founded the Rezayat Group in Kuwait in 1949, which became a major Gulf business conglomerate with over 16,000 employees.
figures.biography:
Abdullah Alireza was a member of the prominent House of Alireza merchant family and was educated in Bombay before beginning his career as secretary for Karl Twitchell, Director of the Saudi Arabian Mining Syndicate. Twitchell had previously been the local representative of California-Arabian Standard Oil Company (CASOC), one of the partners that created Saudi Aramco in 1933. Abdullah became liaison officer for CASOC, serving King AbdulAziz as an interpreter, most notably at the historic ceremony in Dhahran on 1 May 1939, when the King turned the wheel to load the first shipment of Saudi oil.

During World War II, Abdullah was asked to join the Allied administration in Iran with responsibility for distributing essential foodstuffs and supplies throughout the country. On a visit to Kuwait in 1949, he saw opportunities to develop business in the Kuwait-Saudi Arabia Neutral Zone and decided to base his new group in Kuwait. With the help of his sons Teymour and Fahd, he steadily expanded operations into other Gulf states and Saudi Arabia, building important relationships with major Western and Asian contractors that provided a foundation for growth across multiple continents.
achievements: Interpreter for King AbdulAziz at first Saudi oil shipment ceremony (1939)
Founded Rezayat Group in Kuwait (1949)
Built international business conglomerate with 16,000+ employees

figures.name: Ahmed Alireza
figures.title: Business Leader
figures.roleDescription: Third-generation family business leader
figures.significance: Zainal Alireza's grandson who led the family business interests until his death in 2012.
figures.biography:
Ahmed Alireza was the grandson of family patriarch Zainal Alireza and represented the third generation of family leadership in the House of Alireza business empire. He played a significant role in continuing the family's commercial legacy and overseeing the expansion of their business interests across various sectors.

Ahmed died in 2012, after which the family business leadership passed to his surviving children. His sons Khaled, Mohammed, Abdullah, Hisham, Yousef, and his daughter Mariam, along with some of their children including Aamer, Hatim, and Sulaiman, continue to serve as executives for the House of Alireza's commercial interests, including major companies like SISCO, Tusdeer, and Xenel.
achievements: Led third generation of House of Alireza business empire

figures.name: Khalid Alireza
figures.title: CEO of Xenel Group
figures.roleDescription: Current CEO of major family business conglomerate
figures.significance: Current CEO of Xenel Group and son of Ahmed Alireza, leading the fourth generation of family business operations.
figures.biography:
Khalid Alireza is the son of Ahmed Alireza and currently serves as CEO of the Xenel Group, a diversified company based in Jeddah that was founded in 1973 by descendants of the House of Alireza. Xenel, whose name is a spelling variation honoring family patriarch Zainal Alireza, has grown into a major conglomerate with expertise in energy, petrochemicals, construction, infrastructure development, healthcare, industrial services, information technologies, logistics, real estate, and global investing.

As part of the fourth generation of the Alireza family business leadership, Khalid works alongside his siblings Mohammed, Abdullah, Hisham, Yousef, and sister Mariam, as well as other family members including Aamer, Hatim, and Sulaiman, who serve as executives across the House of Alireza's various commercial interests including SISCO, Tusdeer, and Xenel.
achievements: CEO of Xenel Group
Fourth-generation family business leader

figures.name: Zahid Valencia
figures.title: Professional Wrestler
figures.roleDescription: Amateur American freestyle wrestler and NCAA champion
figures.significance: Two-time NCAA Division I national champion and 2025 World Champion in freestyle wrestling at 86 kilograms.
figures.biography: Zahid Valencia was born on May 11, 1997, and is an amateur American freestyle wrestler who competes at 86 kilograms. He attended St. John Bosco High School where he was a three-time CIF state champion, winning four Walsh Jesuit Ironman titles and compiling 158 wins with 100 pins. He was awarded the Dave Schultz High School Excellence Award and named the 2015 Junior Hodge Trophy winner. Valencia committed to Arizona State University in June 2014 alongside his brother Anthony. During his college career from 2016-2020, he became a dominant force in collegiate wrestling, winning two NCAA Division I national championships in 2018 and 2019, and earning three consecutive Pac-12 titles. His senior year was cut short when he tested positive for a recreational substance and was suspended from the ASU wrestling team. In freestyle wrestling, Valencia has achieved remarkable success, becoming a 2025 World Champion at 86 kilograms and a 2023 World Championship medalist at 92 kilograms. He is also a two-time US National and Pan American champion in freestyle wrestling.
achievements: 2025 World Champion at 86 kilograms freestyle wrestling
2023 World Championship medalist at 92 kilograms
Two-time NCAA Division I national champion (2018, 2019)
Three-time Pac-12 Conference champion
Two-time US National and Pan American champion
Three-time CIF state champion in high school
Dave Schultz High School Excellence Award recipient
2015 Junior Hodge Trophy winner

figures.name: Zahid Maleque
figures.title: Politician and Former Health Minister
figures.roleDescription: Bangladesh Awami League politician and former minister of health and family welfare
figures.significance: Former Bangladesh health minister who received WHO award for reducing child mortality and handled COVID-19 pandemic response.
figures.biography: Zahid Maleque was born on April 11, 1959, in Garpara Union, Manikganj Sadar Upazila, Manikgonj District in the then East Pakistan. He completed his undergraduate and postgraduate degrees in English from the University of Dhaka. His father was Colonel M. A. Maleque, a former minister, former mayor of Dhaka, and member of parliament. Maleque built a successful business career, serving as chairman of multiple companies including Sun Life Insurance Company Limited, Bangladesh Thai Aluminium Limited, and several other enterprises. He entered politics representing the Manikganj-3 constituency, first contesting in 2001 but losing to the Bangladesh Nationalist Party candidate. He won the seat in 2008 and was subsequently re-elected in 2014 and 2018. As health minister, Maleque received a WHO award in 2016 on behalf of the health ministry for reducing child mortality. During the COVID-19 pandemic, he faced significant criticism for his handling of the crisis, including issues with protective equipment quality and testing kit availability, with several parliamentary colleagues calling for his removal in June 2020.
achievements: WHO award recipient for reducing child mortality (2016)
Three-term Member of Parliament for Manikganj-3 (2009-2024)
Former Minister of Health and Family Welfare of Bangladesh
Chairman of multiple business enterprises

figures.name: Mustafa Zahid
figures.title: Musician and Singer
figures.roleDescription: Pakistani music composer, singer, and lead vocalist of rock band Roxen
figures.significance: Gained international success with Bollywood soundtracks and is among the top three most-followed Pakistani artists on Spotify.
figures.biography: Mustafa Zahid was born on December 18, 1984, in Lahore, Pakistan. He comes from a notable musical family, being the nephew of Ali Azmat, the former lead vocalist of the famous rock band Junoon, and is also related to the late Sufi kalam singer, actor, director, and producer Inayat Hussain Bhatti. As a child, he never initially planned to become a singer, but his path changed when he was asked to perform at a college event where he sang 'Bheegi Bheegi Raaton Mein,' which motivated him to pursue music professionally. Mustafa formed the rock band Roxen in 2004 and serves as both the band leader and lead vocalist. His breakthrough came in mid-2007 when two of his songs were featured in the Bollywood movie Awarapan, including the hits 'Toh Phir Aao' and 'Tera Mera Rishta.' Since then, he has sung over a dozen Bollywood songs, including major hits in films such as Aashiqui 2 and Ek Villain. Roxen's debut album, Roxen-E-Dewaar, was released in late 2006 and topped charts across South Asia for nearly six weeks. Universal Music signed Mustafa Zahid and Roxen for worldwide album releases, and he has achieved remarkable digital success, becoming one of the top three most-followed Pakistani artists on Spotify with over 5.5 million followers.
achievements: Lead vocalist and band leader of successful rock band Roxen
Singer of major Bollywood hit songs in films like Aashiqui 2 and Ek Villain
Over 5.5 million followers on Spotify, among top three Pakistani artists
Debut album Roxen-E-Dewaar topped South Asian charts for six weeks
Signed with Universal Music for worldwide releases

figures.name: Salma Zahid
figures.title: Canadian Politician
figures.roleDescription: Member of Parliament representing Scarborough Centre in the House of Commons of Canada
figures.significance: First MP to wear a hijab in the Canadian House of Commons and advocate for racialized women's rights.
figures.biography: Salma Zahid was born on May 26, 1970, in Coventry, England, and is a Pakistani Canadian politician. She holds a master's degree in educational management and administration from the University of London's Institute of Education, and an MBA from Quaid-i-Azam University in Pakistan. A mother of two, she has resided in Scarborough since 2000 and is a recipient of the Queen Elizabeth II Diamond Jubilee Medal. Before entering politics, Zahid worked as a community organizer and held multiple positions with the Government of Ontario. She was elected as a Member of Parliament for Scarborough Centre in the 2015 Canadian federal election, succeeding Conservative Roxanne James. During the 42nd Parliament, she served as Vice-Chair for the Standing Committee on the Status of Women, where she was a spokesperson for racialized women. In February 2018, Zahid announced she was taking medical leave to treat Stage 4 Non-Hodgkin lymphoma. During her recovery from chemotherapy, she grew closer to her Islamic faith and began wearing a hijab for personal reasons. In May 2018, she made history by becoming the first MP to wear a hijab in the House of Commons. After completing six rounds of chemotherapy, she was declared cancer-free in July 2018 and returned to her parliamentary duties.
