She was re-elected in the 2019 Canadian federal election but has faced criticism for her positions on international issues, including attending events with Holocaust denialism publishers and calling for support of South Africa's genocide case against Israel in January 2024.
achievements: First Member of Parliament to wear a hijab in the Canadian House of Commons
Vice-Chair of the Standing Committee on the Status of Women
Recipient of the Queen Elizabeth II Diamond Jubilee Medal
Successfully overcame Stage 4 Non-Hodgkin lymphoma
Re-elected Member of Parliament for Scarborough Centre

figures.name: Zahid Nisar Quraishi
figures.title: Federal Judge
figures.roleDescription: United States District Judge and first Muslim Article III federal judge
figures.significance: First Muslim Article III federal judge confirmed by the United States Senate and distinguished military veteran.
figures.biography: Zahid Nisar Quraishi was born in 1975 in New York City and raised in Fanwood, New Jersey, by his parents Shahida P. Quraishi and Dr. Nisar A. Quraishi, immigrants from Pakistan. He graduated from Scotch Plains-Fanwood High School in 1993, received his Bachelor of Arts from John Jay College of Criminal Justice in 1997, and his Juris Doctor from Rutgers Law School in 2000. After law school, he clerked for Judge Edwin Stern and worked as a litigation associate before joining the U.S. Army Judge Advocate General's Corps in 2003. He served two combat deployments in Iraq (2004 and 2006-2007) in Tikrit and Ramadi, providing legal counsel on detainee operations and military justice under intense combat conditions. He was honorably discharged as a Captain, receiving numerous decorations including the Bronze Star and Combat Action Badge. After military service, Quraishi worked as assistant chief counsel at ICE, Assistant U.S. Attorney for New Jersey (2008-2013), and later became a partner at Riker, Danzig law firm. He was appointed as a U.S. Magistrate Judge in 2019, becoming the first Asian-American on the federal bench in New Jersey. On March 30, 2021, President Biden nominated him to be a U.S. District Judge, and he was confirmed by the Senate on June 10, 2021, by an 81-16 vote, making history as the first Muslim-American Article III federal judge. As a judge, he has made notable rulings including blocking New Jersey gun industry lawsuit laws and ending the controversial county line ballot system in New Jersey primaries.
achievements: First Muslim-American to serve as Article III federal judge
First Asian-American federal judge in New Jersey
Bronze Star and Combat Action Badge recipient for Iraq War service
U.S. Army Judge Advocate General's Corps veteran with two combat deployments
Landmark ruling ending New Jersey's county line ballot system
Confirmed by Senate vote of 81-16 in 2021

figures.name: Ahmad Zahid Hamidi
figures.title: Deputy Prime Minister of Malaysia
figures.roleDescription: Malaysian politician, UMNO President, and Deputy Prime Minister
figures.significance: Current Deputy Prime Minister of Malaysia and UMNO President, first UMNO president after independence to not become prime minister.
figures.biography: Ahmad Zahid bin Hamidi was born on January 4, 1953, in Bagan Datuk, Perak, the eldest of nine children to Indonesian-born Malaysian parents. He was notably raised by a Chinese foster-father, Chen Jin Ting, and sold ice cream together for six years during elementary school, an experience he later cited when defending against accusations of being anti-Chinese. He studied at Sekolah Izzuddin Shah and graduated from University of Malaya in 1976, later earning a PhD in Communication from University of Putra Malaysia in 2008. Before politics, he worked as a banker for OCBC and held various corporate positions including directorships at Bank Simpanan Nasional and Permodalan Nasional Berhad. He began his political career in 1986 as political secretary to Najib Razak, was elected UMNO Youth Chief in 1996, and won the Bagan Datok parliamentary seat in 1995, which he has held continuously since. Zahid served in various ministerial positions under Abdullah Ahmad Badawi and Najib Razak from 2008-2018, including Minister of Defence (2009-2013), Minister of Home Affairs (2013-2015), and Deputy Prime Minister (2015-2018). After UMNO's defeat in 2018, he became party president and Leader of the Opposition. Despite leading BN to its worst electoral result in 2022, winning only 30 seats, he successfully negotiated a coalition government with Pakatan Harapan and was reappointed Deputy Prime Minister in December 2022, making him the first person to hold this position twice under different administrations. Throughout his career, Zahid has faced numerous corruption charges, including 87 charges involving RM114 million, though he was acquitted of 40 bribery charges in 2022 and received dismissals on remaining cases in 2023.
achievements: Deputy Prime Minister of Malaysia (2015-2018, 2022-present)
President of UMNO since 2018
Minister of Defence and Home Affairs
Longest-serving MP for Bagan Datok constituency (1995-present)
Leader of Barisan Nasional coalition
Recipient of numerous Malaysian state honors including Dato' Seri titles

figures.name: Issa El-Issa
figures.title: Founder of Falastin Newspaper
figures.roleDescription: Palestinian journalist and newspaper founder
figures.significance: Founded the influential Palestinian newspaper Falastin in Jaffa in 1909, becoming one of the first to warn about the Zionist movement in Mandatory Palestine.
figures.biography: Issa El-Issa was a Palestinian Christian from Jaffa who emerged as a prominent figure in Palestinian journalism in the early 20th century. In 1909, he founded the newspaper Falastin in Jaffa, Palestine, which became one of the most important Palestinian publications of its time. Along with his cousin Daoud El-Issa, he was among the first to warn about the dangers of the Zionist movement in Mandatory Palestine. The El-Issa family became known for their intellectual contributions, politics, and journalism, supporting Palestinian nationalism through their various newspapers and journals.
achievements: Founded Falastin newspaper in 1909
Early warner about Zionist movement in Palestine

figures.name: Daoud El-Issa
figures.title: Palestinian Journalist and Publisher
figures.roleDescription: Newspaper manager and member of Jordan Press Association
figures.significance: Issa's nephew who managed Falastin newspaper and established Al-Bilād in Jerusalem, becoming a member of the Jordan Press Association.
figures.biography: Daoud El-Issa was the nephew of Issa El-Issa and played a significant role in continuing the family's journalistic legacy. He took over the manager position of the Falastin newspaper after his uncle's death, ensuring the continuation of this important Palestinian publication. In 1956, he established Al-Bilād newspaper in Jerusalem, further expanding the family's media influence. His contributions to journalism were recognized when he became a member of the Jordan Press Association in 1976, cementing his role in Arab media circles.
achievements: Managed Falastin newspaper
Established Al-Bilād newspaper in Jerusalem in 1956
Member of Jordan Press Association from 1976

figures.name: Yousef El-Issa
figures.title: Co-founder of Falastin and Publisher
figures.roleDescription: Palestinian journalist and newspaper publisher
figures.significance: Co-founded Falastin newspaper in 1909 and later established Alif Bā' newspaper in Damascus, expanding Palestinian media influence.
figures.biography: Yousef El-Issa was Issa El-Issa's cousin and served as co-founder of the influential Falastin newspaper in 1909. His partnership with Issa was crucial in establishing one of the most important Palestinian publications of the early 20th century. Later expanding his media activities beyond Palestine, Yousef established the Alif Bā' newspaper in Damascus, Syria, which operated from 1930 to 1958. This expansion helped spread Palestinian nationalist ideas and Arab intellectual discourse throughout the Levant region.
achievements: Co-founded Falastin newspaper in 1909
Established Alif Bā' newspaper in Damascus

figures.name: Raja El-Issa
figures.title: First Chairman of Jordan Press Association
figures.roleDescription: Newspaper manager and press association leader
figures.significance: Son of Issa El-Issa who became the first chairman of the Jordan Press Association in Amman in 1956.
figures.biography: Raja El-Issa was the son of Issa El-Issa, the founder of Falastin newspaper, and inherited his father's dedication to journalism and media. After his father's death, Raja took over the manager position of the Falastin newspaper, ensuring the continuation of the family's journalistic legacy. His leadership in the field was further recognized when he became the first chairman of the Jordan Press Association in Amman, Jordan, in 1956. This position made him a pioneering figure in organizing and professionalizing journalism in Jordan, establishing important precedents for press freedom and professional standards in the region.
achievements: Managed Falastin newspaper after father's death
First chairman of Jordan Press Association in 1956

figures.name: Georgette El-Issa
figures.title: Palestinian Philanthropist and Activist
figures.roleDescription: Social activist and philanthropist
figures.significance: Palestinian philanthropist and activist who contributed to social causes and Palestinian civil society.
figures.biography: Georgette El-Issa was a member of the prominent El-Issa family and distinguished herself through her work as a Palestinian philanthropist and activist. While the family was primarily known for their contributions to journalism and media, Georgette carved out her own path in social activism and charitable work. Her efforts contributed to Palestinian civil society and social causes, representing the family's broader commitment to Palestinian nationalism and community development beyond their media enterprises.
achievements: Palestinian philanthropist and activist

figures.name: Mohsin Issa
figures.title: British Billionaire Businessman
figures.roleDescription: Co-founder of EG Group and former CEO of Asda
figures.significance: British billionaire businessman who co-founded Euro Garages (EG Group) and served as CEO of supermarket chain Asda.
figures.biography: Mohsin Issa was born in July 1971 in Blackburn, Lancashire, to Indian Gujarati Muslim parents Vali and Zubeda who had immigrated from Gujarat, India in the 1960s. Growing up in modest circumstances in a terraced house, he was educated at Witton Park Academy. After working briefly in the clothing industry, he co-founded the plastic-packaging firm Europlast in 1993 with his older brother Zakir. In 2001, Mohsin and his brother Zuber co-founded Euro Garages with the acquisition of a single petrol station in Bury, Greater Manchester, which they expanded into a major international business. In October 2020, as part of a consortium with TDR Capital, the brothers became majority stakeholders in British supermarket chain Asda, with Mohsin serving as CEO from August 2021 to September 2024. He was appointed Commander of the Order of the British Empire (CBE) in 2020 for services to business and charity.
achievements: Co-founded EG Group in 2001
Former CEO of Asda
Appointed CBE in 2020
Ernst & Young Entrepreneur of the Year 2018

figures.name: Zuber Issa
figures.title: British Billionaire Businessman
figures.roleDescription: Co-founder of EG Group and owner of EG On The Move
figures.significance: British billionaire businessman who co-founded Euro Garages (EG Group) with his brother and later focused on his separate EG On The Move business.
figures.biography: Zuber Issa was born in June 1972 in Blackburn, Lancashire, to Indian Gujarati Muslim parents who had immigrated from Gujarat, India. He was educated at Witton Park Academy alongside his brother Mohsin. At age 17-18 in 1988/90, Zuber purchased a news kiosk in Preston called Euro News, which he expanded into a newsagent chain in busy mall locations across North-West England. In 1998, he became a shareholder in Europlast, the plastic-packaging firm co-founded by his brothers. In 2001, he co-founded Euro Garages with Mohsin, starting with a single petrol station and building it into a major international business. Initially responsible for strategy and acquisitions while Mohsin handled day-to-day operations, Zuber later stepped back from EG Group to focus on his separate business, EG On The Move. He sold his 22.5% stake in Asda in 2024 to TDR Capital and was appointed CBE in 2020.
achievements: Co-founded EG Group in 2001
Built newsagent chain from single kiosk
Appointed CBE in 2020
Ernst & Young Entrepreneur of the Year 2018

figures.name: Adel Fakeih
figures.title: Former Minister and Mayor
figures.roleDescription: Former Saudi minister and mayor of Jeddah
figures.significance: Prominent Saudi technocrat who served in multiple high-level government positions including Minister of Economy and Planning and Mayor of Jeddah.
figures.biography:
Adel Fakeih was born on July 1, 1959, in Mecca into a family known for its active members in finance and business. His father was a businessman and published poet, and his uncle chairs the Fakeih Group. He obtained a degree in industrial engineering from King Abdulaziz University. Fakeih worked in both private and public sectors, serving as Chairman of Al Jazeera Bank and holding positions on various boards including the Trade and Industrial Chamber of Commerce in Jeddah. From 1993 to 2005, he served as chairman of the Savola Group, Saudi Arabia's biggest food company, and was also chairman of the Jeddah Chamber of Commerce and Industry from 2003 to 2005.

He was appointed mayor of Jeddah in March 2005, launching major development projects including turning the Old Airport into a 12 million m2 city and the Central Jeddah Redevelopment Project. In August 2010, he became Minister of Labor, introducing the Nitaqat program and various labor reforms. He briefly served as Minister of Health in 2014 during the MERS outbreak, then became Minister of Economy and Planning in 2015, helping develop Vision 2030. He was arrested in November 2017 during the Saudi Arabian purge and remained in detention as of 2019.
achievements: Chairman of Savola Group (1993-2005)
Mayor of Jeddah (2005-2010)
Minister of Labor (2010-2015)
Minister of Economy and Planning (2015-2017)
Contributed to development of Saudi Vision 2030

figures.name: Abdulrahman Fakieh
figures.title: Businessman
figures.roleDescription: Saudi businessman detained in 2017 purge
figures.significance: Member of the prominent Al Fakieh business family who was detained during the 2017-2019 Saudi Arabian purge.
figures.biography: Abdulrahman Fakieh is a Saudi businessman from the prominent Al Fakieh family. He was among the businessmen and professionals detained during the 2017-2019 Saudi Arabian purge that began on November 4, 2017. The purge was a mass arrest of prominent Saudi Arabian princes, government ministers, and business people, conducted weeks after the creation of an anti-corruption committee led by Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman. The detainees were confined at the Ritz-Carlton hotel in Riyadh, and as many as 500 people were rounded up in the sweep, with Saudi Arabian banks freezing more than 2,000 domestic accounts as part of the crackdown.

figures.name: Ali Fakieh
figures.title: Embassy Driver
figures.roleDescription: Driver at British Embassy Beirut
figures.significance: Embassy staff member honored with British Empire Medal for services to the British Embassy in Beirut.
figures.biography: Ali Fakieh serves as an embassy driver at the British Embassy in Beirut, Lebanon. He was awarded the British Empire Medal (BEM) in the 2025 Special Honours for his services to the British Embassy in Beirut. The British Empire Medal is awarded for meritorious civil or military service worthy of recognition by the Crown, and is part of the Most Excellent Order of the British Empire. His recognition reflects dedicated service to British diplomatic operations in Lebanon.
achievements: Awarded British Empire Medal (BEM) in 2025

figures.name: Fahd Shobokshi
figures.title: Businessman and Contractor
figures.roleDescription: Present-day contractor and businessman
figures.significance: A prominent Saudi contractor who attended Victoria College, Alexandria alongside future Saudi and Middle Eastern leaders.
figures.biography:
Fahd Shobokshi was a present-day contractor who attended the prestigious Victoria College in Alexandria, Egypt. He was among the schoolmates of Mohammed bin Laden's sons at Victoria College, which served as an elite educational institution for the upper classes of the Middle East. His fellow students included notable figures such as King Hussein of Jordan, Zaid Al Rifai, the Kashoggi brothers, Kamal Adham (who later ran the General Intelligence Directorate under King Faisal), Mohammed Al Attas, Ghassan Sakr, and actor Omar Sharif.

The Victoria College connection placed Shobokshi within an influential network of Middle Eastern elites who would go on to play significant roles in their respective countries' political and economic development. This educational background at one of the region's most prestigious institutions helped establish connections that would prove valuable in business and political circles throughout the Gulf region.
achievements: Attended Victoria College, Alexandria
Established as present-day contractor

figures.name: Hussein Shobokshi
figures.title: Columnist and Media Figure
figures.roleDescription: Former columnist and media commentator
figures.significance: A prominent Saudi columnist and media figure who advocated for government accountability and democratic reforms before being blacklisted.
figures.biography:
Hussein Shobokshi was a prominent columnist for the Saudi newspaper Okaz who became known for his progressive views on governance and social issues. In July 2003, he wrote a controversial column expressing his vision of a country where the government would be fully accountable to the public, citizens could freely vote, and women could drive cars. This article generated significant public reaction, including complaints from what he described as 'tribal and religious groups.'

Following the publication of his progressive column, Shobokshi faced severe consequences for his outspoken views. He was quickly blacklisted for the following year, and his new talk show on the Saudi-owned satellite broadcaster Al Arabiya was cancelled. His editor informed him that he was banned without providing specific explanations about who imposed the ban or the exact reasons. Later, as a Saudi columnist, he praised the television show 'Um Harun' for correcting biased views towards Jewish people, noting that Arabic television viewers, particularly Gulf audiences, were not accustomed to seeing strong Jewish characters unless they were portrayed in villainous roles.
achievements: Former columnist for Okaz newspaper
Advocated for government accountability and democratic reforms
Hosted television show on Al Arabiya

figures.name: Ossama bin Abdul Majed Shobokshi
figures.title: Minister of Health and Diplomat
figures.roleDescription: Former Saudi Minister of Health and diplomat
figures.significance: Served as Saudi Arabia's Minister of Health from 1995 to 2003 and worked as a diplomat.
figures.biography:
Ossama bin Abdul Majed Shobokshi was a prominent Saudi Arabian diplomat and politician who served in key government positions during the 1990s and early 2000s. He held the important portfolio of Minister of Health from 1995 to 2003, overseeing Saudi Arabia's healthcare system during a crucial period of development and modernization in the Kingdom.

In addition to his ministerial role, Shobokshi worked as a diplomat, representing Saudi interests in various international contexts. His career spanned both domestic governance and international relations, making him a significant figure in Saudi political circles. He passed away in March 2023 at the age of 79, marking the end of a distinguished career in public service that contributed to Saudi Arabia's healthcare infrastructure and diplomatic relations during important decades of the Kingdom's development.
achievements: Served as Minister of Health (1995-2003)
Career as diplomat
Contributed to Saudi healthcare system development

figures.name: Abdulla Al-Humaidi
figures.title: Kuwaiti Businessman and Investor
figures.roleDescription: Founder of London Resort Company Holdings and owner of Ebbsfleet United F.C.
figures.significance: Notable Kuwaiti businessman known for major real estate and sports ventures in the UK and Middle East.
figures.biography:
Abdulla Al-Humaidi was born in Kuwait in January 1986 and pursued medical studies at the Royal College of Surgeons in Dublin, Ireland, before obtaining an MBA with distinction from the University of Leicester. Despite his medical training, he chose a business career and established Kuwaiti European Holdings (KEH), expanding into the UK in the early 2010s. In 2013, he acquired Ebbsfleet United F.C. with ambitions to transform it into an English Football League side, though his tenure was marked by financial difficulties including delayed wage payments and the club's eventual relegation from the National League in 2024-25.

In 2014, Al-Humaidi launched the ambitious £3.5 billion London Resort theme park project on the Swanscombe Peninsula in Kent, which was designated a Nationally Significant Infrastructure Project. However, the project faced planning delays and environmental objections, particularly after the site was designated as a Site of Special Scientific Interest in 2021, leading to its collapse into insolvency in 2023. Al-Humaidi was declared bankrupt in the UK in November 2023 and has faced multiple convictions in absentia in Kuwait for fraud and financial misconduct, though he disputes several of these charges.
achievements: Founded London Resort Company Holdings
Acquired and developed Ebbsfleet United F.C.
Established Kuwaiti European Holdings investment firm
Led major real estate developments in Kent, UK

figures.name: Bader Al-Humaidi
figures.title: Member of Parliament
figures.roleDescription: Kuwaiti MP who ran for parliamentary speaker position
figures.significance: Kuwaiti parliamentarian who challenged the political establishment by running for speaker of parliament.
figures.biography:
Bader Al-Humaidi emerged as a significant opposition figure in Kuwaiti politics during the December 2020 parliamentary elections. Following the election in which two-thirds of incumbents lost their seats, 40 MPs jointly announced their intention to vote for Al-Humaidi to replace Marzouq Al-Ghanim as speaker of parliament on December 11, 2020.

During the parliamentary opening session on December 15, 2020, Al-Humaidi challenged the established political order by running against Al-Ghanim for the speaker position. Despite having significant initial support, he ultimately received 28 votes compared to Al-Ghanim's 33 votes, which included 16 votes from cabinet members. His candidacy represented a challenge to the traditional power structure and contributed to the political tensions that characterized the Kuwaiti political gridlock of the early 2020s.
achievements: Garnered support of 40 MPs for parliamentary speaker position
Challenged established political order in Kuwait parliament

figures.name: Bader Saad Mohammed Al Humaidi Al Dosari
figures.title: Lance Corporal
figures.roleDescription: Qatari security official killed in Israeli airstrike
figures.significance: Qatari security official whose death in an Israeli airstrike on Doha marked a significant escalation in regional tensions.
figures.biography:
Lance Corporal Bader Saad Mohammed Al Humaidi Al Dosari was a Qatari security official who became a casualty of regional conflict when he was killed during an Israeli airstrike on September 9, 2025, in Doha, Qatar. The attack targeted residential buildings on Wadi Rawdan Street in the Leqtaifiya (West Bay Lagoon) district, where senior Hamas negotiators working on a ceasefire agreement to end the Gaza war were housed.

The Israeli operation, dubbed 'Operation Atzeret HaDin' (Day of Judgment), involved 10 fighter jets and more than 10 precision munitions, marking the first direct Israeli strike on a Gulf Cooperation Council member state. Al Dosari's death, along with 5 others in the attack, prompted Qatar to condemn the strike as a 'cowardly Israeli attack' and a violation of international law and sovereignty. His death became a significant diplomatic incident that required a formal Israeli apology as one of Qatar's primary conditions for resuming negotiations with Hamas.

figures.name: Ali Sharif Al Emadi
figures.title: Former Minister of Finance
figures.roleDescription: Economist, businessman, and former government minister
figures.significance: Served as Qatar's Minister of Finance from 2013-2021 and was chairman of Qatar National Bank during a period of significant economic growth.
figures.biography:
Born on January 3, 1969, Ali Sharif Al Emadi is a prominent Qatari economist and businessman who held a bachelor's degree in finance from the University of Arizona. He began his career working in the banking control department of the Qatar Central Bank for eight years until 1998, then joined Qatar National Bank where he rose to become chief executive in 2005, succeeding Saeed bin Abdullah Al Misnad.

In February 2013, Al Emadi was included in the Top 100 Powerful Arabs list, ranking 32nd due to his role in the rapid growth and expansion of Qatar National Bank. His banking career ended in June 2013 when he was appointed Minister of Economy and Finance on June 26 following the ascension of Sheikh Tamim bin Hamad Al Thani as Emir of Qatar, replacing Yousef Hussain Kamal. He simultaneously served on the administrative council of the Qatar Investment Authority and as board chairman of Qatar National Bank.

Al Emadi's tenure came to an abrupt end in May 2021 when he was arrested on charges of misuse of public money, abuse of function and abuse of power. He was dismissed from his position as board chairman of Qatar National Bank following his arrest. In March 2023, he was formally charged with bribery, abuse of position and power, damage to public funds and money laundering, for which he will stand trial.
achievements: Appointed Minister of Finance of Qatar (2013-2021)
Served as Chairman of QNB Group
Ranked 32nd in Top 100 Powerful Arabs list (2013)
Led rapid growth and expansion of Qatar National Bank

figures.name: Mohammed bin Abdulrahim Al-Emadi
figures.title: Businessman
figures.roleDescription: Qatari businessman
figures.significance: Notable member of the Al-Emadi family in business sector.
figures.biography: Mohammed bin Abdulrahim Al-Emadi is listed among the prominent members of the Al-Emadi family as a Qatari businessman. While specific details about his business activities and achievements are not provided in the available sources, he represents the family's continued involvement in Qatar's business community.

figures.name: Mohammed bin Ismail Al-Emadi
figures.title: Ambassador and Politician
figures.roleDescription: Qatari Ambassador, politician and businessman
figures.significance: Serves in diplomatic and political roles representing Qatar internationally.
figures.biography: Mohammed bin Ismail Al-Emadi is a prominent member of the Al-Emadi family who has served in multiple capacities for the Qatari government. He has held positions as a Qatari Ambassador, politician, and businessman, representing the family's involvement in Qatar's diplomatic and political spheres.

figures.name: Yousef Hussain Kamal Al Emadi
figures.title: Former Minister of Finance
figures.roleDescription: Former Qatari minister of finance, politician and businessman
figures.significance: Served as Qatar's Minister of Finance before being replaced by Ali Sharif Al Emadi in 2013.
figures.biography: Yousef Hussain Kamal Al Emadi served as Qatar's Minister of Finance until June 2013, when he was replaced by Ali Sharif Al Emadi following the ascension of Sheikh Tamim bin Hamad Al Thani as Emir of Qatar. His tenure as finance minister coincided with a period of significant economic development in Qatar, though specific details about his policies and achievements during this time are not provided in the available sources.
achievements: Served as Qatar's Minister of Finance until 2013

figures.name: Hassan bin Mohammed Rafee Al-Emadi
figures.title: Ambassador and Politician
figures.roleDescription: Qatari ambassador, politician and businessman
figures.significance: Represents the Al-Emadi family in diplomatic and political roles for Qatar.
figures.biography: Hassan bin Mohammed Rafee Al-Emadi is a member of the Al-Emadi family who has served Qatar in diplomatic and political capacities. He has held positions as a Qatari ambassador and politician while also maintaining business interests, representing the family's multi-faceted involvement in Qatar's governance and international relations.

figures.name: Saoud Abdul Aziz M. Al Mohannadi
figures.title: Vice President of Qatar Football Association
figures.roleDescription: Football administrator and FIFA Council member
figures.significance: Prominent football administrator who served as vice president of Qatar Football Association and was elected to the FIFA Council.
figures.biography:
Saoud Abdul Aziz M. Al Mohannadi (1 January 1957 – 10 January 2023) was a prominent figure in Qatari and international football administration. He served as vice president of both the Qatar Football Association and the Asian Football Confederation, and was a member of the FIFA Council. He was elected to the FIFA Council during the Asia Football Confederation meeting held in Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia on 6 April 2019 for the sessions of 2019 to 2023, receiving 37 out of 46 votes along with three other Asian members to represent Asia in the FIFA council.

Throughout his career with the AFC and QFA, Saoud played a key role in organizing major tournaments including the AFC Asian Cup during the 2011 Qatar tournament and 2015 Australian tournament. He served as secretary general of Qatar FA from 2001 to 2012, demonstrating his long-term commitment to football administration in Qatar. Saoud died on 10 January 2023, leaving behind a significant legacy in football governance.
achievements: Elected to FIFA Council (2019-2023)
Vice President of Qatar Football Association
Vice President of Asian Football Confederation
Secretary General of Qatar FA (2001-2012)
Organized AFC Asian Cup tournaments in Qatar (2011) and Australia (2015)

figures.name: Khalil Al-Mohannadi
figures.title: ITTF Deputy President
figures.roleDescription: International table tennis administrator
figures.significance: Deputy President of the International Table Tennis Federation (ITTF) and member of the World Table Tennis selection panel.
figures.biography:
Khalil Al-Mohannadi serves as the Deputy President of the International Table Tennis Federation (ITTF), representing Qatar in international sports administration beyond football. He was appointed to the selection panel for World Table Tennis (WTT) in 2019, a new commercial vehicle for table tennis created by the ITTF. The selection panel, which included ITTF President Thomas Weikert and other senior officials, was tasked with launching WTT to commercialize professional table tennis globally.

As Deputy President of ITTF, Al-Mohannadi plays a crucial role in the governance and development of table tennis worldwide. His involvement in the WTT selection panel demonstrates his influence in modernizing and commercializing the sport, working alongside other international sports administrators to enhance table tennis's global appeal and professional structure.
achievements: Deputy President of International Table Tennis Federation (ITTF)
Member of World Table Tennis (WTT) selection panel

figures.name: Ali Hassan Al-Mohannadi
figures.title: Municipal Council Representative
figures.roleDescription: Elected representative for Al Thakhira constituency
figures.significance: First elected representative for Al Thakhira constituency in Qatar's inaugural municipal elections in 1999.
figures.biography:
Ali Hassan Al-Mohannadi was a prominent political figure who won the inaugural Central Municipal Council elections for Al Thakhira constituency (no. 27) in 1999, marking Qatar's first free municipal elections. He received 34.4% of the vote (65 votes), defeating runner-up Salem Abdullah Al-Shahwani who received 19.3% (42 votes). The voter turnout in his constituency was an impressive 90.5%, reflecting strong community engagement.

Al-Mohannadi successfully retained his seat in both the 2002 and 2007 municipal elections, demonstrating sustained popular support from the Al Thakhira community. His long tenure as municipal representative reflects his dedication to local governance and his ability to represent the interests of the Al Thakhira constituency effectively during the early years of Qatar's democratic municipal system.
achievements: Won inaugural Qatar municipal elections for Al Thakhira constituency (1999)
Retained municipal council seat in 2002 and 2007 elections
Served multiple terms representing constituency no. 27

figures.name: Hamad Lahan Al Mohannadi
figures.title: Municipal Council Representative
figures.roleDescription: Elected representative for Al Thakhira constituency
figures.significance: Elected municipal representative for Al Thakhira constituency in 2011 and retained the position in 2015.
figures.biography:
Hamad Lahan Al Mohannadi became the municipal representative for Al Thakhira constituency when he was elected in the 2011 Central Municipal Council elections, taking over from Ali Hassan Al-Mohannadi who had served for three consecutive terms. He successfully retained his position in the 2015 elections, demonstrating continued support from the local community.

During his tenure, Al Thakhira constituency (which became constituency no. 26 in 2015) included parts of Simaisma, Umm Birka, and Ras Laffan in addition to Al Thakhira itself. His representation coincided with significant development in the region, including infrastructure improvements and heritage preservation projects that were implemented in Al Thakhira during the 2010s.
achievements: Elected to Al Thakhira municipal council seat (2011)
Retained municipal council position (2015)
Represented constituency including Al Thakhira, Simaisma, Umm Birka, and Ras Laffan

figures.name: Rashid Jassim Al-Mohannadi
figures.title: Municipal Council Representative
figures.roleDescription: Elected representative for Al Khor constituency
figures.significance: First elected representative for Al Khor constituency in Qatar's inaugural municipal elections in 1999.
figures.biography:
Rashid Jassim Al-Mohannadi was elected as the first municipal representative for Al Khor constituency (no. 26) in Qatar's inaugural Central Municipal Council elections in 1999. He won decisively with 63.3% of the vote (283 votes), significantly ahead of runner-up Saleh Majed Al-Mohannadi who received 12.3% (54 votes). The voter turnout in Al Khor constituency was 83.4%, reflecting strong community participation.

Al-Mohannadi successfully retained his seat in the 2002 elections, serving during a period of significant development in Al Khor as the city modernized and expanded its infrastructure. His constituency included not only Al Khor city but also areas such as Simaisma, Al Daayen Village, north Lusail, and Ras Matbakh, making him responsible for representing a diverse range of communities in northern Qatar.
achievements: Won inaugural Qatar municipal elections for Al Khor constituency (1999)
Retained municipal council seat in 2002 elections
Received decisive victory with 63.3% of votes in 1999

figures.name: Saqer Saeed Al-Mohannadi
figures.title: Municipal Council Representative
figures.roleDescription: Elected representative for Al Khor constituency
figures.significance: Municipal representative for Al Khor constituency elected in 2007 and retained the position in 2011.
figures.biography:
Saqer Saeed Al-Mohannadi was elected to represent Al Khor constituency in the 2007 Central Municipal Council elections, taking over from Rashid Jassim Al-Mohannadi. He successfully retained his position in the 2011 elections, demonstrating sustained support from the Al Khor community during a period of significant growth and development in the city.

During his tenure representing constituency no. 26 (later no. 25), Al-Mohannadi oversaw municipal governance during Al Khor's expansion related to the nearby Ras Laffan Industrial City and the city's preparation for hosting major sporting events. His constituency included Al Khor city as well as surrounding areas such as Simaisma, Al Daayen Village, north Lusail, and Ras Matbakh.
achievements: Elected to Al Khor municipal council seat (2007)
Retained municipal council position (2011)
Represented Al Khor during period of significant urban development

figures.name: Mohammed Ali Al Mohannadi
figures.title: Cultural Club Founder
figures.roleDescription: Founder of Al Ittihad cultural club in Al Khor
figures.significance: Founded Al Najma cultural club in 1966 (later renamed Al Ittihad), one of three major social and cultural clubs in Al Khor.
figures.biography:
Mohammed Ali Al Mohannadi was a cultural pioneer in Al Khor who founded the Al Najma cultural club in 1966, which was later renamed Al Ittihad. This was one of three significant social and cultural clubs established in Al Khor by the 1960s, alongside Al Asifah and Al Kifah, reflecting the city's growing cultural sophistication during the early oil era.

The club operated a theater in a residential house where they hosted plays addressing various social issues, charging admission fees to attendees. Beyond theatrical performances, Al Ittihad also organized amateur football matches and other cultural activities. Mohammed Ali's initiative in establishing this cultural institution helped foster artistic expression and community engagement in Al Khor during a transformative period in Qatar's history.
achievements: Founded Al Najma cultural club (1966, later renamed Al Ittihad)
Established theater programming addressing social issues
Organized cultural performances and amateur football matches

figures.name: Issa bin Ali Abu Jamhour Al Mohannadi
figures.title: Merchant and Contractor
figures.roleDescription: Founder of first local contracting company in Al Thakhira
figures.significance: Founded the first local contracting company in Al Thakhira in the 1930s, responsible for building many local houses, wells and mosques.
figures.biography:
Issa bin Ali Abu Jamhour Al Mohannadi was a pioneering businessman who established the first local contracting company in Al Thakhira in the 1930s, during the early period of Qatar's modernization. His company played a crucial role in the development of Al Thakhira's infrastructure, taking responsibility for constructing many of the village's essential buildings and facilities.

His contracting company was instrumental in building numerous local houses, wells, and mosques throughout Al Thakhira, contributing significantly to the community's physical development during a time when such local expertise was rare. His entrepreneurial initiative helped establish indigenous construction capabilities in the region, reducing dependence on external contractors and fostering local economic development during the pre-oil era.
achievements: Founded first local contracting company in Al Thakhira (1930s)
Built numerous houses, wells and mosques in the village
Pioneered local construction industry in the region

figures.name: Suhail Bahwan
figures.title: Omani conglomerate industry executive
figures.roleDescription: Business executive and conglomerate leader
figures.significance: Notable Omani business executive who led a major conglomerate in the region.
figures.biography:
Suhail Bahwan was an Omani conglomerate industry executive who played a significant role in the business landscape of Oman and the broader Gulf region. He was associated with major business operations and industrial development in the country.

Bahwan's influence extended beyond traditional business operations, as he was connected to various sectors of the Omani economy. His leadership in the conglomerate industry helped establish important business networks and partnerships that contributed to Oman's economic development. He passed away in 2025, leaving behind a legacy of business leadership in the region.
